# [The Short (and Long) of Trump's Trade War](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=163343487&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzMzQzNDg3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwMDgwODMsImV4cCI6MTc0OTYwMDA4MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4pULfzXArn6E0O8ADSYgYgqGNj7MftYpX8IG35F_A_c)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0e49172f-a5f7-45b2-bae7-0db3bee2a282?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As the global trade war continues unabated, each new day brings headlines sounding the alarm over its impact. Deciphering how various policies could impact specific industries is no trivial task and requires thinking about developments through multiples perspectives across various geographies and timelines.
And as we're all aware, there is a long list of products that are being impacted, crucial items such as pharmaceuticals, rare earths, energy, technology, and...tomatoes. Yes, tomatoes. This might not be on the top of your list, but it is certainly at the top of mine. As I have recently written, I run a frozen pizza manufacturing company, and tomatoes - used for sauce - are one of our most vital ingredients. And while it is probably not the sexiest topic to cover when discussing the impacts of the trade war, it is still illustrative of the trade dynamics driving the debate and buffeting the global economy.
* * *
## [Practicing What I Preach](https://substack.com/redirect/22546e0f-54d1-4819-91e9-8d6aa6c15d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Mar 17
[](https://substack.com/redirect/22546e0f-54d1-4819-91e9-8d6aa6c15d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[For years, I've suggested that the best way to navigate uncertainty is to integrate thinking by crossing silos and connecting dots. I've often described this method as the "generalist approach" becau...](https://substack.com/redirect/22546e0f-54d1-4819-91e9-8d6aa6c15d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/22546e0f-54d1-4819-91e9-8d6aa6c15d76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
Americans primarily get their tomatoes from two places: California and [Mexico](https://substack.com/redirect/9bebc529-1b30-420b-90b8-d145c070c1b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). California is the largest domestic source of tomatoes in the U.S., and over[ 90%](https://substack.com/redirect/eaafd1e9-636c-4ced-98b4-6abd8723aa1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of America's processed tomatoes comes from the state. In 2023, the state produced over [11 million tons](https://substack.com/redirect/374309b5-5afa-40cd-9d52-33ebbe366dad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of tomatoes.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..gtXPT-cYqtAIaYDLN1rpzNJcuSk50GN9gnhkjSxadUU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163343487)
Mexico is the largest foreign supplier of tomatoes to the U.S., accounting for [90%](https://substack.com/redirect/01512b7e-9698-49ae-adac-15c4f50ba6c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of our tomato imports. This is especially important in the off-season in the U.S., when the U.S. increases its buying of tomatoes from Mexico, where tomatoes can be grown year-round.
**Americans primarily get their tomatoes from two places: California and[Mexico](https://substack.com/redirect/9bebc529-1b30-420b-90b8-d145c070c1b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
As you would expect, it is cheaper to grow tomatoes in Mexico; this is for any number of reasons, but chief among them is labor costs. According to a University of Florida [study](https://substack.com/redirect/286537c1-5b10-40ee-9fac-d78fdcb44fb6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), farmworkers in Mexico earned $1.20 per hour versus a much higher $14.11 in the U.S. These higher costs result in U.S. tomatoes [costing](https://substack.com/redirect/c1caaf5d-842f-4993-a3a7-81dd154d02b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) somewhere between $.90-$1.80 per pound, whereas Mexican tomatoes [cost](https://substack.com/redirect/8d758080-545c-4acf-9bf1-c1ddc161031b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) around $.41 per pound.
As a result, not surprisingly, [70%](https://substack.com/redirect/d53ab376-7ca3-463e-8f1c-73e719c7047d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the tomatoes bought in the U.S. are from Mexico.
But the trade war is changing all of that. The U.S. Department of Commerce has [imposed](https://substack.com/redirect/9bebc529-1b30-420b-90b8-d145c070c1b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a nearly 21% tariff on Mexican tomatoes, which is expected to go into effect in July. This has already had an impact in Mexico, which is seeing declining exports and job losses. And while this first order effect is clear and obvious, it's the next link in the chain of global interconnectedness that illustrates how it might impact Americans.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e17a6444-93d7-4254-b22d-d989eedf056f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo by [Roychan Kruawan](https://substack.com/redirect/c7d1c949-4519-4402-b9a4-dd15acc87bd0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on [Unsplash](https://substack.com/redirect/1be01b0a-96ce-40b1-a5a7-eeb1b18b140c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
If Mexican tomato prices rise, demand for Mexican tomatoes will fall. Assuming no shift in consumer preferences, this will (on the margin) lead to higher demand for American tomatoes. In the short-run, this likely means paying more for salsa, salads, and yes, tomato sauce, leading some analysts to sound [alarm bells](https://substack.com/redirect/c915fb09-4fe2-4de7-9486-34d7c3ac4b4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over what could be additional inflation pressures for the American consumer.
Which brings me back to [Goodwell Foods, the company I currently run](https://substack.com/redirect/090638e0-ffd6-40a2-8f45-feeb91887a19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). We make millions of pizzas a year - sold in major retailers throughout the U.S. - and we need tomatoes for our sauce. But here's the wrinkle. In the U.S., tomatoes are primarily used for three things: ketchup, salsa, and sauce. The tomatoes used for salsa come mostly from Mexico, whereas those used for [sauce](https://substack.com/redirect/0c0008be-fa59-4750-9ca9-32154d95274a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) come primarily from [California](https://substack.com/redirect/9bebc529-1b30-420b-90b8-d145c070c1b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So, while salsa-makers are going to have problems, pizza companies like mine should be fine, right?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..gtXPT-cYqtAIaYDLN1rpzNJcuSk50GN9gnhkjSxadUU?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163343487)
Not so fast. If salsa-makers can't get enough tomatoes from Mexico, they're going to have to get them from somewhere else. California will be the first place they look, and the increased demand is going to increase prices.
Even though I don't use tomatoes from Mexico, my costs will still increase, and if the tomatoes that are used in our sauce are more expensive, then our product is going to be more expensive. We pride ourselves on being able to feed a family of four for only a few dollars - no small feat in these inflationary times - but that will be harder for us to do if the cost of our ingredients goes up. And if we sell fewer pizzas as a result, then we won't have to employ as many people, we'll hire fewer truck drivers to move our products, and the ripple will flow through to less economic activity. That means less pay for workers, who will be squeezed with less money and higher costs.
The pain we're almost certain to feel in the short run is real. We need to expect a slower economy (even if only temporarily) as uncertainty and higher costs paralyze decision-makers and decrease activity. **The long-run story, however, could be different. Let 's consider a possible future. **
Higher tomato prices in America will spur California (and other) farmers to grow more, leading to an increase in domestic production. All else equal, additional supply will lower prices - but with the very important long-term impact of reducing our imports. And while the price impact is unclear, it's probably not going to be as high as the tariffs suggest and may even lead to lower prices.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c9f4fd7a-527e-4a51-a1a5-1b3f310d107b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: EOS Data Analytics, [https://eos.com/blog/how-to-grow-tomatoes/](https://substack.com/redirect/a9759626-43c8-408c-a54f-588a93b7cd7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This is of course part of the motivation behind the trade war...and the tariffs have the potential to help lots of industries...and ultimately, American consumers. _**By raising the cost of imports, tariffs can stimulate more domestic production, reduce our trade deficits, and create jobs for American workers**_. And while this outcome is far from certain, it's important to highlight it's a very real possibility.
Amidst all the gloom and doom of today's trade war, **is it conceivable there 's a bright future ahead**? I've often advised the best way to navigate uncertainty is by thinking of various potential future**s,** and with the disproportionate focus these days on the costs, it's worth at least considering the benefits. _So what will happen?_ Only time will tell, but in the meantime, one thing remains certain: something as seemingly trivial as tomatoes can have a big impact on a small business in a small town in a small state.

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/8e8f5da6-2450-417b-9d90-b3e18e8e4b1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/dfb46468-a102-4fcf-8544-26d4671909da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/4aeaa55c-f191-4688-827a-09d79eb336c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

Even though I don't use tomatoes from Mexico, my costs will still increase, and if the tomatoes that are used in our sauce are more expensive, then our product is going to be more expensive. We pride ourselves on being able to feed a family of four for only a few dollars - no small feat in these inflationary times - but that will be harder for us to do if the cost of our ingredients goes up. And if we sell fewer pizzas as a result, then we won't have to employ as many people, we'll hire fewer truck drivers to move our products, and the ripple will flow through to less economic activity. That means less pay for workers, who will be squeezed with less money and higher costs. The pain we're almost certain to feel in the short run is real. We need to expect a slower economy (even if only temporarily) as uncertainty and higher costs paralyze decision-makers and decrease activity. The long-run story, however, could be different. Let 's consider a possible future. Higher tomato prices in America will spur California (and other) farmers to grow more, leading to an increase in domestic production. All else equal, additional supply will lower prices - but with the very important long-term impact of reducing our imports. And while the price impact is unclear, it's probably not going to be as high as the tariffs suggest and may even lead to lower prices. | | ---|---|--- Source: EOS Data Analytics, This is of course part of the motivation behind the trade war...and the tariffs have the potential to help lots of industries...and ultimately, American consumers. By raising the cost of imports, tariffs can stimulate more domestic production, reduce our trade deficits, and create jobs for American workers. And while this outcome is far from certain, it's important to highlight it's a very real possibility. Amidst all the gloom and doom of today's trade war, is it conceivable there 's a bright future ahead? I've often advised the best way to navigate uncertainty is by thinking of various potential futures, and with the disproportionate focus these days on the costs, it's worth at least considering the benefits. So what will happen? Only time will tell, but in the meantime, one thing remains certain: something as seemingly trivial as tomatoes can have a big impact on a small business in a small town in a small state. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Kelan Publishing, LLC PO BOX 932, Lincoln, NH 03251 ? ?

name: Playlist
subject: Now Playing: Free Bird by Lynyrd Skynyrd
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Orden for Congress
body:
🎶 And this bird you cannot change 🎶

| |
---
| | | Peter,

Whenever I need to get pumped up, my go-to song is **" Free Bird" by Lynyrd Skynyrd. **
_🎶And this bird you cannot chaaaa-aa-aaange 🎶_
I play it in my office in DC, and when it's nice out, I like to open up the windows so everyone walking by can jam out to it too.
_🎶 Lord knows I can't chaaa-aa-aaange 🎶_
I played it onboard one of the original WWII C-47s while wearing a 1942 paratrooper uniform right before jumping into France to commemorate the 80th anniversary of D-Day.
---
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/PFlkpyI7vh_bkS8DtSP7olY8lZ6eQEXZIJAmorm0lqZDWZQb7J3HRiOt-o7ppy4oTR3sOTTE6ri4UxkKI1NunzgREjwlPJw0Tvp2oasNIqyvr5wJ6YIh4CEPf5BfsGK57OzkJaD8SU8TPxnhuvOTZaBqOC60l9kePqX6koE980Ke442EBfsyzW3SthTcpYOvPoEsMn0aYm5CcRBsyNiG2fU-p2qUDCGofX4SVawE7pT_ss8Gx8vKcSpTDmVPbWbbIAQcbj_AAtoaM96D2Qn4Ab4hQnviepzkHpOo4eQ3r5tmDnq3Es70XNSiBsrV-OeRuS1fI3kxJ-GssFEqNWSCSt5CYAjdhcSS5-SB1rnvMbmsQ9JEu6RlY_vRJlxUSGm18-E4Kof40W-bCC7AkFsXqDQAdfT3R_vk7rHN9kRJWKQ6t47gx87QWh3EGvd-xjcPZtlEFtFhLsH1nEzm7zsUr8pTs9EzPbJfSwCBxCxH7a4iPxh9o5qbt4nATLv7tzgNMLKoMUnmbg/oOPXh1bRzjKY3PrMzNBjeRwKBAD7MLHa/8 "Derrick playing ")

**I tell you this because I 'm going to get a lot of use out of this song over the next year and a half. **

I know these emails can seem constant, and trust me - _it 's my least favorite part of the job._ But let me remind you what I'm up against...
1\. _George Soros_ is funding protests in my district and funneling his dark money to beat me.
2\. My opponent raised _$1 MILLION in her first 20 days_ of announcing.
3\. The Democrat Super PAC tasked with winning the House has _$50 MILLION_ to take me out.
_**Do you see why I need to get pumped up, and why I need YOUR help, Peter?**_

clean_body: 🎶 And this bird you cannot change 🎶 | | | | | Peter, Whenever I need to get pumped up, my go-to song is " Free Bird" by Lynyrd Skynyrd. 🎶And this bird you cannot chaaaa-aa-aaange 🎶 I play it in my office in DC, and when it's nice out, I like to open up the windows so everyone walking by can jam out to it too. 🎶 Lord knows I can't chaaa-aa-aaange 🎶 I played it onboard one of the original WWII C-47s while wearing a 1942 paratrooper uniform right before jumping into France to commemorate the 80th anniversary of D-Day. | "Derrick playing " I tell you this because I 'm going to get a lot of use out of this song over the next year and a half. I know these emails can seem constant, and trust me - it 's my least favorite part of the job. But let me remind you what I'm up against... 1\. George Soros is funding protests in my district and funneling his dark money to beat me. 2\. My opponent raised $1 MILLION in her first 20 days of announcing. 3\. The Democrat Super PAC tasked with winning the House has $50 MILLION to take me out. Do you see why I need to get pumped up, and why I need YOUR help, Peter? "Donate today so a Retired Navy SEAL, grandpa, and Lynyrd Skynyrd-loving Congressman can hold a district to stand up for folks like us!" | | "DONATE ANY AMOUNT!!" Every dollar the Democrats raise goes towards lying about me, my record, and my values. Every dollar you donate today will be used to stop the liberal money machine and get the truth out about me. I was raised by a single mother who had to use food stamps to put food on the table. On my campaign, you can trust that I run a tight budget and every single penny is well-spent.I know you work hard for your money, and I don 't take it lightly when you choose to donate whenever you can afford it. | | "DONATE TODAY >>" If you can 't donate today, as always, please keep my family and me in your prayers. I know I'll do the same. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 5/11/2025
body:
Legislative Session Ends. What Passed, What’s Next, and a Special Mother’s Day Message
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**May 11, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
First and foremost—**Happy Mother’s Day** to all the moms, grandmothers, and mother figures reading this. Today we celebrate your strength, your sacrifice, and your irreplaceable role in shaping the next generation. In a world that often tries to devalue motherhood, we want to say loud and clear: _you matter._
Now, onto a major milestone: **[the 2025 Colorado legislative session officially adjourned this week](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** , capping off one of the most aggressive and ideologically driven sessions we’ve seen in years. Over the next several days, we’ll be rolling out coverage across our social channels and newsletter feeds as Governor Polis begins signing—or vetoing—the final wave of bills.
Before we dive in, just a reminder that tickets are now on sale for our **Mountain Majesty Gala featuring Eric Trump!** Don’t wait—tickets are going fast for what promises to be an unforgettable evening celebrating Colorado’s conservative spirit and grassroots momentum. Join us June 21st for dinner, speakers, and a night to remember. [Grab your tickets now before they’re gone!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
As for the legislature? Let’s call it what it was: **a progressive power grab.**
Democrats pushed through a $44 billion budget—$3 billion larger than last year’s—despite warnings of an economic downturn. They cut transportation funds and gutted local budgets while ramping up spending on controversial social programs and expanding government control across nearly every sector.
**[Here’s just a glimpse of what passed:](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
* HB25-1312, targeting parental rights and free speech by redefining discrimination law.
* SB25-003, the gun permit bill that drew national protest.
* SB25-276, doubling down on Colorado’s sanctuary state status.
* HB25-1327, aimed at restricting conservative ballot initiatives.
* HB25-1309, forcing insurance to cover gender-affirming care, including for minors.

And then there were the education bills—limiting book challenges, silencing dissent at school boards, and funneling even more state oversight into classrooms.
Yes, the session is over. But the impact is just beginning. Governor Polis now has a stack of bills on his desk—and we’ll be watching closely as he begins signing (or vetoing) them in the coming days.
Read our full breakdown of what happened this session here:
**[🚨 The Good, the Bad, and the Alarming: What You Need to Know from Colorado’s 2025 Legislative Session](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
We’ll be rolling out full coverage across our social channels and daily newsletter updates throughout the week. Stay tuned, stay engaged, and know this: _you’re not alone._
In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Michelle Chandler stopped a predator — and uncovered victims who may never know](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On May 11, 2024, at Nordstrom Rack in Lakewood, Colorado, Michelle Chandler caught a man filming her inside a women’s fitting room. She didn’t freeze or panic. Instead, she confronted him, pinned him to the ground and held him until help arrived – or so she thought.

**[“Parents Last”: Senate Democrats Advance HB25-1312 Despite Mass Opposition, Custody Concerns](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado’s controversial “Kelly Loving Act” is one step away from becoming law, after the state Senate advanced HB25-1312 in a party-line vote Monday night. The bill passed 23-12 following hours of floor debate—nearing an end to a legislative saga that’s drawn over 700 would-be testifiers, more than 17,000 emails from concerned constituents, and ongoing warnings from legal experts, parents, and educators.

**[Members left in the dark: LPEA board spends big while margins shrink and bills climb](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When La Plata Electric Association (LPEA) members open their May power bills, most will see the effects of a 7.72% rate increase that quietly took effect April 1. While LPEA’s board says the hike is needed to cover infrastructure and supply costs, many members are beginning to ask harder questions – not just about what they’re paying, but about how their cooperative is being run.

|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
**[Backlash ignored: Senate approves HB25-1312 without parental rights protections](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) After weeks of public backlash, failed compromise efforts, and a marathon Senate floor debate, Colorado lawmakers gave final approval Tuesday to HB25-1312 – a bill that critics say severs parents from decisions about their children’s identities in school.

**[Enos: If parents can’t challenge books or protect embryos, who will?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CHRISTIAN HOME EDUCATORS OF COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The majority in the Colorado General Assembly seems to have caught the attention of the Trump Administration. The U.S. Department of Education spokeswoman Julie Hartman told the Daily Signal that “Children do not belong to the government. They belong to parents.”_

**[Money matters: Colorado lawmakers pass bill adding financial literacy to graduation requirements](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[POST INDEPENDENT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado just became the 27th state to turn financial literacy courses into a graduation requirement after the bill passed the Senate with a vast majority vote on Wednesday.

**[“YIGBY” bill to let churches build housing on their land fails without Senate support](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The campaign known as “YIGBY” – “Yes in God’s Backyard” – to allow churches, school districts, colleges, and universities to build affordable housing on their land failed in the waning days of the Colorado legislative session.

**[READ MORE LEGISLATIVE SESSION UPDATES](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**STATE**
**[A turning point for Colorado: RMV announces first Mountain Majesty Gala featuring Eric Trump on June 21](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The conservative grassroots in Colorado is waking up – and gathering for something bigger than politics as usual. Tickets are now on sale for what organizers are calling a major turning point for Colorado conservatives.
**[Boebert presses FBI, joins Trump and allies rallying to free ‘political prisoner’ Tina Peters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Rep. Lauren Boebert is demanding federal action on behalf of Tina Peters, the former Mesa County Clerk sentenced to nine years in prison over her efforts to preserve election records following the 2020 election.
**[CPW tracks four suspected wolf dens, ranchers brace for more uncertainty](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[POST INDEPENDENT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) For wolves, the beginning of May signals the end of denning season. While Colorado Parks and Wildlife is tracking up to four pairs of wolves that could be denning, none have been confirmed, according to Eric Odell, the agency’s wolf conservation program manager.
**[Colorado Senate rejects judicial discipline appointee over misconduct cover-up ties, approves another](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**

What Passed, What’s Next, and a Special Mother’s Day Message | | | | May 11, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear Readers, First and foremost—Happy Mother’s Day to all the moms, grandmothers, and mother figures reading this. Today we celebrate your strength, your sacrifice, and your irreplaceable role in shaping the next generation. In a world that often tries to devalue motherhood, we want to say loud and clear: you matter. Now, onto a major milestone: , capping off one of the most aggressive and ideologically driven sessions we’ve seen in years. Over the next several days, we’ll be rolling out coverage across our social channels and newsletter feeds as Governor Polis begins signing—or vetoing—the final wave of bills. Before we dive in, just a reminder that tickets are now on sale for our Mountain Majesty Gala featuring Eric Trump! Don’t wait—tickets are going fast for what promises to be an unforgettable evening celebrating Colorado’s conservative spirit and grassroots momentum. Join us June 21st for dinner, speakers, and a night to remember. As for the legislature? Let’s call it what it was: a progressive power grab. Democrats pushed through a $44 billion budget—$3 billion larger than last year’s—despite warnings of an economic downturn. They cut transportation funds and gutted local budgets while ramping up spending on controversial social programs and expanding government control across nearly every sector. HB25-1312, targeting parental rights and free speech by redefining discrimination law. SB25-003, the gun permit bill that drew national protest. SB25-276, doubling down on Colorado’s sanctuary state status. HB25-1327, aimed at restricting conservative ballot initiatives. HB25-1309, forcing insurance to cover gender-affirming care, including for minors. And then there were the education bills—limiting book challenges, silencing dissent at school boards, and funneling even more state oversight into classrooms. Yes, the session is over. But the impact is just beginning. Governor Polis now has a stack of bills on his desk—and we’ll be watching closely as he begins signing (or vetoing) them in the coming days. Read our full breakdown of what happened this session here: We’ll be rolling out full coverage across our social channels and daily newsletter updates throughout the week. Stay tuned, stay engaged, and know this: you’re not alone. In liberty and truth, Heidi Ganahl | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK On May 11, 2024, at Nordstrom Rack in Lakewood, Colorado, Michelle Chandler caught a man filming her inside a women’s fitting room. She didn’t freeze or panic. Instead, she confronted him, pinned him to the ground and held him until help arrived – or so she thought. Colorado’s controversial “Kelly Loving Act” is one step away from becoming law, after the state Senate advanced HB25-1312 in a party-line vote Monday night. The bill passed 23-12 following hours of floor debate—nearing an end to a legislative saga that’s drawn over 700 would-be testifiers, more than 17,000 emails from concerned constituents, and ongoing warnings from legal experts, parents, and educators. When La Plata Electric Association (LPEA) members open their May power bills, most will see the effects of a 7.72% rate increase that quietly took effect April 1. While LPEA’s board says the hike is needed to cover infrastructure and supply costs, many members are beginning to ask harder questions – not just about what they’re paying, but about how their cooperative is being run. | LEGISLATIVE SESSION After weeks of public backlash, failed compromise efforts, and a marathon Senate floor debate, Colorado lawmakers gave final approval Tuesday to HB25-1312 – a bill that critics say severs parents from decisions about their children’s identities in school. The majority in the Colorado General Assembly seems to have caught the attention of the Trump Administration. The U.S. Department of Education spokeswoman Julie Hartman told the Daily Signal that “Children do not belong to the government. They belong to parents.” Colorado just became the 27th state to turn financial literacy courses into a graduation requirement after the bill passed the Senate with a vast majority vote on Wednesday. The campaign known as “YIGBY” – “Yes in God’s Backyard” – to allow churches, school districts, colleges, and universities to build affordable housing on their land failed in the waning days of the Colorado legislative session. | STATE The conservative grassroots in Colorado is waking up – and gathering for something bigger than politics as usual. Tickets are now on sale for what organizers are calling a major turning point for Colorado conservatives. Rep. Lauren Boebert is demanding federal action on behalf of Tina Peters, the former Mesa County Clerk sentenced to nine years in prison over her efforts to preserve election records following the 2020 election. For wolves, the beginning of May signals the end of denning season. While Colorado Parks and Wildlife is tracking up to four pairs of wolves that could be denning, none have been confirmed, according to Eric Odell, the agency’s wolf conservation program manager. The Colorado state Senate on Wednesday rejected the reappointment of the chairwoman to the state panel that handles judicial discipline but narrowly kept its vice-chair. | NATIONAL Colorado’s Democratic lawmakers are pushing ahead on immigration bills despite the U.S. Department of Justice filing a lawsuit Friday to challenge those laws. The chiming of the Vatican’s church bells confirmed for the faithful gathered in St. Peter’s Square that a new successor to the See of St. Peter the Apostle has indeed been chosen. | LOCAL A building paid for with bingo money. At an April school board meeting near Colorado Springs, debate raged over a proposed policy to ban transgender students from playing on school sports teams that match their gender identity. | COMMENTARY In our previous devotional, we explored righteousness as God’s unwavering standard for building just and right “equable” relationships—between us and God, and with all creation. clean_body: Want to make sure everyone is filled in | = | Hey there! We know that there has been a lot going on in the news these last few weeks, so we wanted to give you a quick recap: FIRST : Republicans announced their target seats to flip from blue to red — and no single state has more targeted districts than California. THEN : We responded with tremendous grassroots enthusiasm. Lots of lots of supporters stepped up to fund our plans to defend Democratic U.S. House seats. This support gives us the resources we need to open regional offices, hire organizers, print literature, and more. NEXT : Republicans saw our fundraising momentum and decided to attack our ability to raise grassroots dollars. Donald Trump issued an executive action putting the Democratic donation processing tool ActBlue in the crosshairs. NOW : = = = = = = Thank you for being in this fight! \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. If this email was forwarded to you, We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, And if you no longer wish to receive emails from the California Democratic Party, click here to . | | | | ---|---|---|--- | Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www.cadem.org | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Peter,
I didn’t run for office 5 years ago to become famous or get invited to swanky cocktail parties in Washington, D.C.
I ran because I was sick and tired of seeing our country speeding towards a cliff with career politicians from both parties at the wheel, and I decided to do something about it.
Then I got to Congress and saw firsthand just how corrupt and broken our government really is, and let me tell you, one of the biggest hurdles we face in our movement to save America is the U.S. Senate.
**The worst of them is Jon Ossoff, or as I like to call him, Radical Transgender Activist Jon Ossoff.**
Ossoff is already working behind the scenes to impeach and remove President Trump from office, he even admitted it at his recent town hall.
**He’s even running his re-election campaign as if I’m already his opponent****, smearing me every chance he gets, and using my name to fundraise from liberal online donors.**
**_[That’s right... Jon Ossoff can’t raise money without using MY NAME](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/cGjZh9PEAFghYj3kJYeipNKNCX3SSNc7vfeWK_JQiUA5we3fcs0pTa9RWQmk4K-RRszDpr_ndoWmrMEAOHrtfJRQlPrcvoYFTXtMxSM67GDjvfzg_hIpJ0kX8P33VgSlilQqmIxQFON0RCBpDReyVJP7oVf_c-vTmK6gP00KPmg4sVcrcyPGljDrGK2LNgn9IZZI_pLhwvHyAl8p5heUzjwgC3xC3_m-pUZVdbTp3u7rK_IZ5UlBtZi8qeLxzCl03dMSwyosgZ3X4O08Eh7vEXm25cXdgMUbmgUsoexUtvBIhrhAQ-yBv_vxYC5WMH-6szTPObDgetg1z-3AukB_ARbmZs4Ogfuzb3IV1AAABED2nmKNsW61SRPxxYIJ1xRjn5jyfDnXLbdoxJ1kmqbsMppminMy3xida9nu_PPs85sdx0BvlnM3XM-iNJejFl5h78cFEka-64zuPPF5A6na/I7xnHYPX2lINsh9rS2GBlTPu5x7j_Q3b/8)_.******
**[He is obviously terrified of me running for Senate... So maybe that’s exactly what needs to happen???](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/tW5tTOp82zbIerKplsQnovZPRKk9gumfyEtpl_MJfRpKuPmlJKute8vPpcwBlekZ58wqu1dnM2ilpVqe9MYp1pe-vyC8F6UrAdAlbtAsQpxAO_LwHRyZfL1O7W-TEh-V0Bo6eDkRg-3Kt_EOksMeaPDeh_iF6saL_94mMkZnfUxbLPaXNF8JEfACSPtYDS1fED0pAmburjTa0Al26YIa_ieXebcMo0Drb__t2B4tB9pWDTKO4slYCAHLdq9qfBqnYLi_VWPUFT2DfN4-7XiAKhaCvT5BhTIS9qa2Sm0CH5SX0WJZbCDximjneRFAGDfmk0H0tbYnpW_zFOo6udetwI2iPu6gsQ-aSVBvIWP4VCsis33tcVywffzdK2Z-efuB96ZjV3Xvb90bgvctVZeVCD3Cq-yzdlKEcgL0oEiPtix7l991G_sJMakti3SVqggAy1dh-BkbJX5JdNXD5abJ/Y0q4_g4noDP9a5gdRWSc60TkrJwtGcBn/8)**
Peter: Before anything further happens - Before I move to the next step - **_[I need to hear from you](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/XqXCwbC0MguiJsA0zkMBEezDQ-1KV7dIIHFRT4WXOwM74LqszPlvdFPiqsVo4Y1hpynxBwEuJMMPGiIEZPzF-gVyway5cPnzOQvg60cqsf-hpwcYRPm9JeBHFHOVMx-llgeb7FOPasMg9a2R2JdSfftAunrX4XI2mmtpg_qVVTrvz8xBSWGmwSmqvEaY9sc-h8nlifBcn_4SuBx-AXR92E9yhUKQgZOVph2yeR9zWwgwK4PPIW4yUeGQV2E2K01ZoT_MugEFZT6hI2ZdMjBseNUJATqheXpRWrJxluXXC-63hZLNtXmZImoXCeA9qLiskU53M2JFM2hdTakQasJEC65JmZjIDaMlZShpJBxEmzuUPzRP9Yp8V26dP2zOKoyx_ibFivjcC9PIZbb8XdW6t_urx8aQL8uI5AphgyHEw2SsDXWCBNOeKSQ8utM7AxX738tkj1iLo3qsLYPMQfhW/7DY7-gq2cRcybl9Zq3Gy0Ea_TsN3xj9S/8)_.**
**I need to know if I should run for Senate in Georgia, and if I should I need to know if I will have your support or not.**
This would be the biggest fight of my life, the most important, the most brutal, and the most high-stakes.
_The radical Left_ ,_the fake news media_ , _the RINOs_ , _the globalist billionaires_ \- they would all come after me with everything they’ve got.
But I’m not afraid of them. I’m only afraid of running this race without the full support of the America First patriots who have had my back since day one...
**[So I need you to tell me now:](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/KOCJVVr9O9z9Wxfv5R25wtlWCimYw6pEjy3qSAgcKciCEuXkzcEl8X7tnNMGqB5MvNtyT7fgu65BaagpeNcsG54pSLTOhCDqhjXPAbKUW1xZ-2zhSXNc9L1YL6Q_9tKTkhY0OqeohFO-Z9ge6JqAoYgS-h3b6cT3qpkdB-H3Dh2fQFA1IYLUFqw3vPv6nO7vDGXmBZqtaSLNc7JxjFM2zcWgyjuiJCBg9BGWjAjSCjuM8pj_WJMF79ypTlR6Ht6mo5nMlKY8nMRi3zb6L6IIrSCarJ43YMnVZIHgJwOmAxf7XXPkTqqkiFnbZ6zpKkZtE50CK0793jL3wVTdZPFqTmeBFNkJ8sPFrtPVNeKwYWgJxOJXGe_mYhVIPQ6gcQBmTstzclQcVIBRs3aCUHu25uFxBchfQru0_78DrBpR2ZDZnBO7dZJOqKfFwCD6nSlzhtZlnzfPis-7kmvufLZ4/AYEpTrTKYpDVwpdPNj81h9VBZFgGzLpc/8)**

Happy Mother's Day! Really, I mean it.
And congrats on the presents. We all have a lot to celebrate.
Embracing peace and repudiating war was the original vision to which Mother's Day was dedicated....We all received a few gifts this weekend, whether we each knew it or not.
### What is Mother's Day, anyway?
Most people think of Mother's Day as a day to celebrate mothers and motherhood, which makes a great deal of sense.
But today's holiday is also much more than that. Among other things, it's a day to _show solidarity_ with mothers.
Embracing peace and repudiating war was the original vision to which Mother's Day was dedicated. I discussed that history when we last celebrated today's holiday last year:
## [Mother's Day means more than celebrating mothers](https://substack.com/redirect/4ead704a-1e6b-4506-90be-3fd91f947f4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| May 12, 2024
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4ead704a-1e6b-4506-90be-3fd91f947f4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[My family lost our mom to cancer in 2016, which led me to dedicate my thanks on today's holiday to the mothers among my friends, siblings, and in-laws.](https://substack.com/redirect/4ead704a-1e6b-4506-90be-3fd91f947f4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/4ead704a-1e6b-4506-90be-3fd91f947f4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Last week, when I noticed today's holiday looming on the calendar, I recall feeling melancholy, disappointed that the day to celebrate mothers and peace would coincide with so many armed conflicts seeming to emerge in every corner of the world.
But we all received a few gifts this weekend, whether we each knew it or not.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=163355238&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzMzU1MjM4LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwMzI5MDEsImV4cCI6MTc0OTYyNDkwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8b0BOTJALA_J1FUVQd8NYHLAaxUtUjkItJBuDxW6dCE)
### A Mother's Day present for everyone from the Vatican
The succession of Pope Francis represented a fraught moment in the history of the Catholic Church. The Cardinals tasked with choosing his successor had the opportunity to turn the clock back on progress, or instead embrace it--which they did in a particularly important way.
Pope Leo XIV is poised to [continue the humanitarian legacy](https://substack.com/redirect/8a427eeb-2fed-422a-8914-f0b812191bc3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Pope Francis, with the added significance of being the first Pope to speak with an American voice. As a global leader from the United States offering a counterpoint to the madness emanating from Washington, Pope Leo might be poised to help redeem Americans in the eyes of the world.
Time will tell. But I remain grateful for his selection nonetheless, and--having been a [vocal fan](https://substack.com/redirect/8a3789a7-93a8-4ef3-9cec-b9d100b9fd46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of his predecessor--feel some excitement by Pope Leo's papacy.
### A Mother's Day present for everyone--especially South Asians
Science fiction writers often invoke the specter of World War III looming in the indefinite future. But, in the same way that most Americans overlooked [longstanding bipartisan fascism](https://substack.com/redirect/d235d3d5-759e-405c-937e-8bd9d842b149?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the past, might today's observers be failing to recognize [the World War that appears to have already begun](https://substack.com/redirect/c579383b-2d38-4d36-bd2e-8f8c476f4285?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in several theaters around the globe?
[decolonizing.love](https://substack.com/redirect/81efbe73-c21b-4222-87db-378b8dfcf0fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/9a8a2c67-1c97-4446-8aeb-44ff06a06d0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A post shared by [@decolonizing.love](https://substack.com/redirect/81efbe73-c21b-4222-87db-378b8dfcf0fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Few of the many international conflicts underway across the world carried the same destructive potential as the one between India and Pakistan, even if only because both countries hold nuclear weapons.
Over the past three years, international observers have feared that the war between Russia and Ukraine could easily escalate, implicating not only the entangling alliances that enabled World War I, but also the devastating use of nuclear weapons. Eastern Europe, however, is not the only region where nuclear armed adversaries are killing each other's citizens over territorial disputes.
My native country, Pakistan, has gone to war on [three separate occasions](https://substack.com/redirect/d211e872-6974-4178-bdeb-e44d6a8ef2c6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with its neighbor India over the course of the 75 years since the two countries gained independence from the former British empire. In 1999, President Bill Clinton infamously referred to the disputed territory between them as the "[most dangerous place](https://substack.com/redirect/9bdd9923-8cf6-41b6-9ae7-339a21eb6974?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" on Earth.
That place grew a lot more dangerous in the past week, and threatened the possibility of expanding conflict well beyond it.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/559ac714-d572-43fb-bcc7-a163e510bff9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The most recent escalation of the long simmering conflict between Indian and Pakistan appears to have started with [attacks in Pahalgam, Kashmir on April 22](https://substack.com/redirect/d1f03552-91e0-4451-a0eb-ba8705247888?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that killed 26 tourists. The Indian government blamed the attacks on militants allegedly sponsored and trained by Pakistan.
In retaliation, India launched [missile strikes last Wednesday, May 7](https://substack.com/redirect/10bf8be4-18ce-4ac5-a834-f86902a7f308?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), targeting half a dozen locations in and well beyond Kashmir, [killing](https://substack.com/redirect/117d12de-cff2-4b7b-b6c4-d8106f39fabc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over two dozen civilians and injuring many more. The same day, India and Pakistan fought one of the [largest air battles](https://substack.com/redirect/9c507fbe-8351-4605-b338-8a7f476ef0f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since the Second World War, with 125 planes fighting at ranges of up to 100 miles.
A series of [drone strikes](https://substack.com/redirect/0adfe87a-ee6a-47ee-adfd-2a0b8f8bfaa3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the following day, while likely the work of the Pakistani military, was [disclaimed](https://substack.com/redirect/b4706572-9570-4e99-9d6f-462d691cb01e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the government. In addition to reviving the specter of escalation between the two nuclear armed countries, and killing at least 50 people, that escalation also presented the further danger of [information warfare](https://substack.com/redirect/9e55f7c4-3ed5-4336-b627-c9ecab69dc1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..uCYwW-aPDtkTDmqbnR755DhGpaPSYQKQch63V4AORr8?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=163355238)
### Neighbors in arms
The last time the two countries formally went to war was 26 years ago. At the time, the conflict was focused on a particularly inaccessible region in Kashmir named Gilgit. On the one hand, the region has a substantial population of over 1 million people. On the other hand, it also includes over 50 peaks rising to over 23,000 feet, among which the 1999 conflict was primarily fought.
For the sake of comparison, the tallest mountain in the United States (excluding Alaska) is Mount Whitney in California, which rises to 14,500 feet.
In other words, the 1999 conflict between India and Pakistan was fought largely over a region in which people cannot usually survive without oxygen supplementation, suggesting that was as much about lines on a map and resources as it was over any conception of self-determination, identity, or rights.
In contrast, last week's conflict embroiled population centers, including Muzzafarbad, the capital of the part of Kashmir administered by Pakistan, and Indian cities including Srinagar and Amritsar--the site of the Sikh Golden Temple, which the Indian government [bombed in 1984](https://substack.com/redirect/82a014ca-d8a1-4f12-beee-f30109c54b74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### Another unaccountable military industrial complex
In 2007, I traveled across Pakistan with a delegation of lawyers from across the United States to write [a report for Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/513cbbb3-1963-4f22-a1d2-3027a307fe04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the unfortunate (and unfortunately predictable) support for dictatorship enabled by U.S. foreign policy.
In the time since then, the country has continued its long pattern of vacillation between autocracy and democracy. I was in the capital city on the day when former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was [assassinated](https://substack.com/redirect/f008403b-41b4-4049-aa20-ccb28a47bb55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and happened to be in a meeting with a sitting justice of the country's Supreme Court at the precise time when news broke of her killing.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=163355238&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzMzU1MjM4LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwMzI5MDEsImV4cCI6MTc0OTYyNDkwMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.8b0BOTJALA_J1FUVQd8NYHLAaxUtUjkItJBuDxW6dCE)
Since then, the military's attacks on, and intimidation of, the country's civilian leadership has predictably continued. Former Prime Minister Imran Khan was the subject of a [long running military campaign to discredit him](https://substack.com/redirect/9e1de157-3d3e-4da1-bc86-d51504e2e467?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (seemingly at the [behest](https://substack.com/redirect/a6aad247-786a-4a30-8871-1444840ed073?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the Biden administration), before ultimately being [removed](https://substack.com/redirect/ff738b89-bb94-410f-81e6-dbf9524f1a22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from office and subjected to a politically motivated prosecution.
The idea of Pakistan initiating a new conflict with India while hiding its actions and seeking plausible deniability by using paramilitary proxies would be entirely on brand for the country's military leadership, but there is so little transparency into its machinations that I can only offer an analysis of patterns fueling my suspicions, more than facts that remain illusive at this point.
For now, I'm writing simply to make sure my readers can place the escalating security threats around the world in an expanding context. From Ukraine to Gaza, all the way to the Indian subcontinent, it seems the Third World war might already be in full swing.
Especially in that context, the [ceasefire](https://substack.com/redirect/0c3faf91-c949-4b9d-b23f-130ca0ffb8e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) announced yesterday is a gift to the world. I chafe at the suggestion that any U.S. officials deserve any credit for it, but still find the news worthy of celebration.
_Paid subscribers can access further sections including a poem by an Arab poet reflecting on the uniquely tragic losses endured by Palestinian mothers, and a recent news report that appears to bring to life a premise well-established in science fiction..._

clean_body: A holiday formed as a plea for peace coincides with a ceasefire in one theater of an emergent world war, and a global role for an American leader standing in antithesis to the president | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A holiday formed as a plea for peace coincides with a ceasefire in one theater of an emergent world war, and a global role for an American leader standing in antithesis to the president | | | May 12| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Happy Mother's Day! Really, I mean it. And congrats on the presents. We all have a lot to celebrate. Embracing peace and repudiating war was the original vision to which Mother's Day was dedicated....We all received a few gifts this weekend, whether we each knew it or not. What is Mother's Day, anyway? Most people think of Mother's Day as a day to celebrate mothers and motherhood, which makes a great deal of sense. But today's holiday is also much more than that. Among other things, it's a day to show solidarity with mothers. Embracing peace and repudiating war was the original vision to which Mother's Day was dedicated. I discussed that history when we last celebrated today's holiday last year: | | | | ---|---|--- | May 12, 2024 | Last week, when I noticed today's holiday looming on the calendar, I recall feeling melancholy, disappointed that the day to celebrate mothers and peace would coincide with so many armed conflicts seeming to emerge in every corner of the world. But we all received a few gifts this weekend, whether we each knew it or not. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post. A Mother's Day present for everyone from the Vatican The succession of Pope Francis represented a fraught moment in the history of the Catholic Church. The Cardinals tasked with choosing his successor had the opportunity to turn the clock back on progress, or instead embrace it--which they did in a particularly important way. Pope Leo XIV is poised to of Pope Francis, with the added significance of being the first Pope to speak with an American voice. As a global leader from the United States offering a counterpoint to the madness emanating from Washington, Pope Leo might be poised to help redeem Americans in the eyes of the world. Time will tell. But I remain grateful for his selection nonetheless, and--having been a of his predecessor--feel some excitement by Pope Leo's papacy. A Mother's Day present for everyone--especially South Asians Science fiction writers often invoke the specter of World War III looming in the indefinite future. But, in the same way that most Americans overlooked in the past, might today's observers be failing to recognize in several theaters around the globe? A post shared by Few of the many international conflicts underway across the world carried the same destructive potential as the one between India and Pakistan, even if only because both countries hold nuclear weapons. Over the past three years, international observers have feared that the war between Russia and Ukraine could easily escalate, implicating not only the entangling alliances that enabled World War I, but also the devastating use of nuclear weapons. Eastern Europe, however, is not the only region where nuclear armed adversaries are killing each other's citizens over territorial disputes. My native country, Pakistan, has gone to war on with its neighbor India over the course of the 75 years since the two countries gained independence from the former British empire. In 1999, President Bill Clinton infamously referred to the disputed territory between them as the "" on Earth. That place grew a lot more dangerous in the past week, and threatened the possibility of expanding conflict well beyond it. | | ---|---|--- The most recent escalation of the long simmering conflict between Indian and Pakistan appears to have started with that killed 26 tourists. The Indian government blamed the attacks on militants allegedly sponsored and trained by Pakistan. In retaliation, India launched , targeting half a dozen locations in and well beyond Kashmir, over two dozen civilians and injuring many more. The same day, India and Pakistan fought one of the since the Second World War, with 125 planes fighting at ranges of up to 100 miles. A series of the following day, while likely the work of the Pakistani military, was by the government. In addition to reviving the specter of escalation between the two nuclear armed countries, and killing at least 50 people, that escalation also presented the further danger of . Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. Neighbors in arms The last time the two countries formally went to war was 26 years ago. At the time, the conflict was focused on a particularly inaccessible region in Kashmir named Gilgit. On the one hand, the region has a substantial population of over 1 million people. On the other hand, it also includes over 50 peaks rising to over 23,000 feet, among which the 1999 conflict was primarily fought. For the sake of comparison, the tallest mountain in the United States (excluding Alaska) is Mount Whitney in California, which rises to 14,500 feet. In other words, the 1999 conflict between India and Pakistan was fought largely over a region in which people cannot usually survive without oxygen supplementation, suggesting that was as much about lines on a map and resources as it was over any conception of self-determination, identity, or rights. In contrast, last week's conflict embroiled population centers, including Muzzafarbad, the capital of the part of Kashmir administered by Pakistan, and Indian cities including Srinagar and Amritsar--the site of the Sikh Golden Temple, which the Indian government . Another unaccountable military industrial complex In 2007, I traveled across Pakistan with a delegation of lawyers from across the United States to write about the unfortunate (and unfortunately predictable) support for dictatorship enabled by U.S. foreign policy. In the time since then, the country has continued its long pattern of vacillation between autocracy and democracy. I was in the capital city on the day when former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto was , and happened to be in a meeting with a sitting justice of the country's Supreme Court at the precise time when news broke of her killing. Since then, the military's attacks on, and intimidation of, the country's civilian leadership has predictably continued. Former Prime Minister Imran Khan was the subject of a (seemingly at the of the Biden administration), before ultimately being from office and subjected to a politically motivated prosecution. The idea of Pakistan initiating a new conflict with India while hiding its actions and seeking plausible deniability by using paramilitary proxies would be entirely on brand for the country's military leadership, but there is so little transparency into its machinations that I can only offer an analysis of patterns fueling my suspicions, more than facts that remain illusive at this point. For now, I'm writing simply to make sure my readers can place the escalating security threats around the world in an expanding context. From Ukraine to Gaza, all the way to the Indian subcontinent, it seems the Third World war might already be in full swing. Especially in that context, the announced yesterday is a gift to the world. I chafe at the suggestion that any U.S. officials deserve any credit for it, but still find the news worthy of celebration. Paid subscribers can access further sections including a poem by an Arab poet reflecting on the uniquely tragic losses endured by Palestinian mothers, and a recent news report that appears to bring to life a premise well-established in science fiction... | MassGOP Statement on Entrance of Brian Shortsleeve Into 2026 Race for Governor
---
---
| BOSTON, MA -- Massachusetts Republican Party Chair Amy Carnevale responded to Monday's announcement by Republican Brian Shortsleeve that he is entering the 2026 gubernatorial race.
"The MassGOP welcomes Brian Shortsleeve into the race for governor. Our growing field of strong gubernatorial candidates will give voters the best opportunity to choose who they want to prosecute the failures of the Healey-Driscoll administration. The excitement we are seeing at the top of the ticket is also being reflected down ballot as voters demand better accountability and transparency from legislators," said Carnevale.
###

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
subject: Can I explain why I’m running?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
body:
Because if we want to truly revive this country, we have to lead from the ground up — with bold, America First governors who won’t flinch in the face of the administrative state.

[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMCIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/dp9Dye2MXrtC-Zajnz6dxg)Hi, it’s Vivek.
You’ve probably heard by now: I’m running for Governor of Ohio. _But I want you to hear why — directly from me._
We’re living through a pivotal moment in American history.
President Trump’s decisive re-election was more than a political comeback — it was a national gut-check. A repudiation of the corrupt ruling class. A mandate to finally take back control of our country.
I was proud to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with President Trump on the frontlines: Turning out Gen Z voters for the GOP, rallying grassroots patriots, and building up the America First movement brick by brick.| [](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMSIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/fLfLBjRNuM3fbDT5n9980Q)| [](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMiIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/bMDnOKJm01J79_C3AWctnA)
---|---
**[As President Trump leads at the federal level, the fight moves to the states — and I’m stepping up to lead it in Ohio.](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiMyIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/pSLuRnK_xX8D8LO3xGVUIw)**

Because if we want to truly revive this country, we have to lead from the ground up — with bold, America First governors who won’t flinch in the face of the administrative state.

This is my home. I was born here. I built companies here. Apoorva and I are raising our two boys here. And I believe Ohio can become the model for conservative excellence — a state that leads the nation with strength, vision, and unapologetic American pride.
[](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiNCIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/8wQWVsqFTjagniT9tgZ4NQ)
But that means doing things differently than they’ve always been done. I’m not running to manage the status quo. I’m running to disrupt it.

Here’s what that looks like:
1. **Tell folks the truth** – no lies, no gimmicks, no political speak
2. **Support Trump 100%** – he will have no better partner at the state level
3. **Overhaul failing schools** – I’ll expand school choice and reward excellent teachers
4. **Restore national identity** – we’ll promote merit and patriotism, and reject victimhood
5. **Deport every illegal alien** – Tom Homan is a hero, he’ll have a direct line to my office
6. **Roll back transgenderism** – ban men in women’s sports, no sex changes for minors
7. **Slash taxes and lower prices** – you earned your nest egg, you should keep more of it
8. **Gut all DEI programs in the state** – we’ll be a model for rooting out woke propaganda
9. **Secure elections and prosecute fraud** – our elections will be the safest in the country
10. **Obliterate the managerial bureaucracy** – I’ll take a hacksaw to government bloat

I get it – that’s not everyone’s cup of tea. Some folks would rather settle for a liberal Democrat or a go-along-to-get-along Republican. That’s not me. And it’s not the campaign I’m running.

**[I’m in this because we need a fighter – someone with the spine to take on the Democrat machine and the Deep State – right here in battleground Ohio.](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiNSIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyZleF90aWQ9MjAyNTA1MTJfQUQtUTEuMTE0NTEwX3QxNjM4NzQzLTM2MjMifQ/yt3hff1xqph3pnRZfelwYQ)**

And we can’t just win. We need to crush them.

**We need a landslide victory that sends a message across the nation – just like President Trump did in 2024.**

That’s where you come in:

_Whether you live in Ohio or not, will you make a contribution of ANY amount today? _

Not just to hit a goal, but to send a message: You’re with us. You believe in this movement. And you’re ready to fight.
**Choose your impact:**
| [**CONTRIBUTE $4** ](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6OTE4ODYyNjYsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiMzYyMy1kcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJxIjoiMDItYjI1MTMyLTZkZDM2MDMwNmI2YTRmNGU5ZDA2MzkxZTViOTA2OWYzIiwibSI6ZmFsc2UsInVpIjoiNiIsInVuIjoiIiwidSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vc2VjdXJlLndpbnJlZC5jb20vdml2ZWstcmFtYXN3YW15LWZvci1vaGlvL3doeS1zZi8_cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTQmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMjUwNTEyX0FELVExLjExNDUxMF90MTYzODc0My0zNjIzJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDUxMl9BRC1RMS4xMTQ1MTBfdDE2Mzg3NDMtMzYyMyJ9/uA0UHdNQC8oxuS0AzD7koQ)

**[Every dollar you chip in helps me build the kind of grassroots army it will take to win the most important race in America in 2026.](https://trk.emails.dailygopnews.)**

I’m not doing this for a political career. I’m doing this to get the job done — and leave this country stronger for the next generation.

Let’s make Ohio the leader of America’s revival.

Thank you — and God bless the United States of America.

**Vivek Ramaswamy**

This is my home. I was born here. I built companies here. Apoorva and I are raising our two boys here. And I believe Ohio can become the model for conservative excellence — a state that leads the nation with strength, vision, and unapologetic American pride. But that means doing things differently than they’ve always been done. I’m not running to manage the status quo. I’m running to disrupt it. Here’s what that looks like: 1. Tell folks the truth – no lies, no gimmicks, no political speak 2. Support Trump 100% – he will have no better partner at the state level 3. Overhaul failing schools – I’ll expand school choice and reward excellent teachers 4. Restore national identity – we’ll promote merit and patriotism, and reject victimhood 5. Deport every illegal alien – Tom Homan is a hero, he’ll have a direct line to my office 6. Roll back transgenderism – ban men in women’s sports, no sex changes for minors 7. Slash taxes and lower prices – you earned your nest egg, you should keep more of it 8. Gut all DEI programs in the state – we’ll be a model for rooting out woke propaganda 9. Secure elections and prosecute fraud – our elections will be the safest in the country 10. Obliterate the managerial bureaucracy – I’ll take a hacksaw to government bloat I get it – that’s not everyone’s cup of tea. Some folks would rather settle for a liberal Democrat or a go-along-to-get-along Republican. That’s not me. And it’s not the campaign I’m running. And we can’t just win. We need to crush them. We need a landslide victory that sends a message across the nation – just like President Trump did in 2024. That’s where you come in: Whether you live in Ohio or not, will you make a contribution of ANY amount today? Not just to hit a goal, but to send a message: You’re with us. You believe in this movement. And you’re ready to fight. Choose your impact: | | | | I’m not doing this for a political career. I’m doing this to get the job done — and leave this country stronger for the next generation. Let’s make Ohio the leader of America’s revival. Thank you — and God bless the United States of America. Vivek Ramaswamy Candidate for Governor of Ohio | | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201 To unsubscribe from Vivek Ramaswamy for Ohio,

## Texas Oil Reserves Estimate Increased
The U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) recently announced that vast, [previously undiscovered oil and natural gas reserves](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OhCAURp8GOw1c8YeCYhu72XZLw8BlvBsUA4zOvP3GbfYBtvqKk5wvOV-3-WYo0Pb4NCvq8vJx3vDMAUvBNK8xYaSQMGM6MLv4TduBudAMHLqad7WA2RkK71lWTt-5U6JCLQY5cDn0Y1ctWvXKC4lWGgnDNb0fvbN8HKVCvKuK9CXjnQAhuABoWnEflVQce2l46zyTHB2VmJqyYMGXyT6844GpsXGtgl5K2TNrPxhMDKbzPBu7pLhdlMH0eAZf22hyYTCZVMgGZDA9c_1H6ufmKNvLia6OFGrgrVCqhWZf9ippt58UAuV-YJI_VkPh97v8r9ih4ScAAP__GnSBJA) lie beneath East Texas and Louisiana. The Hosston and Travis Peak formations contain an additional 28 million barrels of oil and nearly 36 trillion cubic feet of natural gas compared to previous estimates--enough to [fuel the nation's current rate of consumption for 14 months](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1qwzAUBeCnsTcZSZZ_Mmgooc6UUCiho5Gtq_gWWQrSjZO8fUmHvkC3Awc-DufrOB4NegyXk1lB08PFMcA9eyCCNK4xQUSfIEPaINv4jWGDTDhKLhvGGybkaA3656hKqydud6IELTrVcdW1fVMu2k1mtlLM3EEn-qmvZTspa93EGyVdX5eoXxhvhBSCCymrWkz9Tu04tMrw2rpCcbBIMVW0AMHDZOefcYNUzXEtvV6Irrmo3wo5FHKI6K8JZ3iVhRzeA6TL8y-wAwRIxhdyOAeLeX45YNnBZIaBnT_Z4eYd20eTie3jzVv2Ee-Q2MkQxsBcTEwodoyBllwttPoyaXu9o_eY265Q_LIa9L_L6H9_blr-BAAA__8Gq4w9). To date, the formations have produced [126 million barrels of oil and 8 trillion cubic feet of natural gas](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOpCAUBeCnwY4JXFG0oNjGbrbd0qBcHTYIhovO-PZ_nFf4m9Oc5MvJ-fccn9YHH9e_dkNTPksaI74pYCmYxy1lTD5kJMwnkkv_fTyRih9BQMNFwyWMzvpwjapyZhKulxUaqZUWSrddU71M3QpRd20PnYa51jC1yzxZVXdW4yIbW3lzY6KRIKWQAI9aTl2veoGtsqJ2C1MCnS8pP8oLC34sLeFKJ-bHnLYqmFcpO7H6D4OBwTAlzLinXO6WwXDjDAbxDc1g2FPBWLwNfEmZH9F5mm8NHU8-cBsdXy3xjJSOPCNxH_lBfD3CwudkqXC7YpwvTvYiBkOVjdvfPgRPrWZKrJv14Tut_O7Q08BPAAAA__-9M4r6). The natural gas deposits, classified as "tight gas" trapped in dense rock at depths up to 10,000 feet, are accessible due to extraction techniques refined over the last few decades. "_The shift to horizontal drilling with fracking has revolutionized oil production, and we 've changed with it_," [said USGS geologist Christopher Schenk](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL9uwyAQh5_G3rAAg50MDF28pWtHC4fDvgpDxBE7efuKSn2CTqdP9-n-_L5u881iwLh-2h1Mefk0RzgpQCmQ5z1lSBgyEOQDyKVvjAdQwVlyqRnXTMjZWQzvWbXOLNxdRQtGjGrkahwuut3M4DVchAC1LHKEwXktPMh-UL3XYtCqRVOHcS2kEFxI2fViuVzVlcOgLO-dbxQHhyXlrmxQ4GXJh3c6IHf3tLfBbKU8qOk_Gjk1cjrPs3vSSt2ajkZO9ZtabMEUbWCVWYYAlqCRUzX_kJglAqIdYmHP6JDudQs4ljAwGx1bbZUpPfMdiG2JqKRYO2027nFiCEjD2Ci-7hbD73nlf6EeRv4EAAD__4mhkD4). ## Texas High School Legends Honored in Waco
Nine former Texas high school football players and coaches were [inducted into the Texas High School Football Hall of Fame](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O5CAQhJ_GznoEmPFPQHCJs730QgubxvQtpkfA2jNvf5rVvcEmJZVKKn1Vfz6WD0uR0v7bHmjq0_OS8CoRa8W8HJyRKWYsmE8sjv9SOrFUWpRQdxB3kGpxluJr0a0zq3CTbNHIQQ9CD_14b4PRKAaxiU5s4yRRjcMwTsKvvVe-w16NLZl3mbhLJaWQSt06uY6TngT22orO-UYLdFQ532rAik9bfHzxifm28dFGE2p9lKb71ai5UfN1XbfP5_N8h42a32saNUfebIT_hhK8-CtDQtrDyjkwu0bNmVfMFfZM3lMCIgKbHDxszbR9wmEDH1iAkvvaKjqgVBm-eSDQHqBsgTmCZ66rjRHCW9iDtwe22bjHRTFS6YdGi_2wFL_5689eP436FwAA___k6Js-) in Waco on Saturday. Among the honorees were Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes, 2011 Heisman Trophy winner Robert Griffin III, and UTSA head coach Jeff Traylor. Mahomes, 29, started two seasons at Whitehouse High School. [As a senior](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkk-P2yAQxT8Nvq0FA07igw9Rq0g9pOqtR2tixma6GCJg8-fSz16FVGqrVU974TePeTwEmu_H8YjsOSxfcaWh3OY4BrpmT6VQGteYKLJPlCldKNv4g8OFcuERJHQvsntRMFpkfx9NY4eTtL1qaFBbs5Vmu9l1jRtwB0YZaXoNHWhNOM94UtJqLa2dABseHmGyU6CUVACtVqddb3pJG4NS21kYSZZLTG1xVOiGefb3eKHUTnFt_OBKOWeh9wIOAg4U2iu_8pksYxvTIuDw0AIO37Aknl7HI7q4Uhaghd7_FHpf6FaE_vwlCOhAzjGWE3r_EJ_qjqMn0Faa2tmovqoz5sxhqfUdk81_znXynaPEt8nZeA1_2Xqzq0xv2f0nCcPzatW9c_6bKPE36popcEy15OfjHC_u2ZpcjL5t0mDPV_ae82YrjFxWZF__tXxsGi4D_AoAAP__jXi8Kw), he threw for 4,619 yards and 50 touchdowns while rushing for 948 yards and 15 touchdowns. During his [two seasons as the starting quarterback](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkj-P3CAQxT8N7s7in9e3hYtTok22uERKk9LCZrxMgsGCOXu3yWePFq8UnZRUaZg3A-_3hOD7a_9q0GO4fDEzdHSdYh9gyx6IIPVzTBDRJ8iQVsg2_sCwQibsJZfNE2-ehOytQX_rdWW7gdujqKATrW65bg_PTeW6VvCmFWbSthm0OQzjcByAj63W42QGYSrs7jDeCCkEF1LWSgzPR33kcNCGKzsxzcEixVSTA4KryZO_xRVSPca58p0jWjJTL0yemDxBqDf8iQtYNHVMFyZP957J07c4QKL-U8JpwtCfz2cmFVMvv5h6IbgSUx-_rpCYbCQnB3vdYqkZTI4h3_WHMnjsp7fswBY5xd1qdmMk44uKUymyeMVBlO5mks378bDblXz43kZn47Zn8c2h35MWkzOGy7soVZiqaf_B1OKvTCRXxLGsGAjSCAthDLkuo8975AaBHoB3WB8z_bkTz2RoN4zOzMsd43DJdZU6u2zoPeZDyzS_zAZ9eTP6v5-2dvJ3AAAA__-koNfe), he led the Bulldawgs to consecutive state championship games, throwing for 3,357 yards and 41 touchdowns while rushing for 2,161 yards and 32 touchdowns. The Hosston and Travis Peak formations contain an additional 28 million barrels of oil and nearly 36 trillion cubic feet of natural gas compared to previous estimates--enough to . To date, the formations have produced . The natural gas deposits, classified as "tight gas" trapped in dense rock at depths up to 10,000 feet, are accessible due to extraction techniques refined over the last few decades. "The shift to horizontal drilling with fracking has revolutionized oil production, and we 've changed with it," . The USGS began looking for previously untapped resources 50 years ago during the Arab oil embargo. New UT Civics School Receives $100 Million The University of Texas at Austin has announced a to renovate a laboratory building to accommodate its new School of Civic Leadership, established in 2023. The school can trace its origins to the Liberty Institute, by conservative donors and Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick to combat the perceived influence of critical race theory in higher education. “ We need to get back on the pathway of ensuring that we are educating our students with the leading concepts that have led to the great country we are ," during a Thursday ceremony to announce the development. The college enrolled its first students last fall. In a few months, 100 freshmen will join the program. The renovation is scheduled to be completed in 2028. Among the honorees were Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes, 2011 Heisman Trophy winner Robert Griffin III, and UTSA head coach Jeff Traylor. Mahomes, 29, started two seasons at Whitehouse High School. , he threw for 4,619 yards and 50 touchdowns while rushing for 948 yards and 15 touchdowns. He went on to play for Texas Tech, leading the country in passing yards and total touchdowns. Griffin, 35, played for Copperas Cove High School. During his , he led the Bulldawgs to consecutive state championship games, throwing for 3,357 yards and 41 touchdowns while rushing for 2,161 yards and 32 touchdowns. He then played four seasons for Baylor. "When I go around the league, all I brag about is Texas high school football.
