name: Left Action
subject: Energy Star has saved consumers billions of dollars. Trump wants to cut it.
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell Trump: Don’t get rid of Energy Star! >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4f9808da-2f5f-434e-92bb-a055997bf80e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6ab93c6e-2ea4-4027-9c79-5dd8ad53ddb4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Trump is trying to get rid of Energy Star, the enormously popular program that saves Americans about $40 billion a year in energy costs, and has broad and wide support from consumers and industry groups alike. Energy Star was created by George H.W. Bush and later signed into law by a Republican Congress. The program certifies over 50 different types of household appliances based on energy efficiency, and has saved consumers about $500 billion over the past 3 decades – just by helping people make energy-efficient choices on their own. There's absolutely no reason for Trump to cut this successful and well-known program. He's just being vindictive and hostile to any government program that actually helps the American people. [**Join us in demanding Trump leave Energy Star alone! >> **](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0a4715ce-02f8-486a-9d58-c98b1961544d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

name: Congressman Jason Crow
subject: I’m with Michael
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act]
Hi friend,
It’s Congressman Jason Crow.
My colleague and friend Senator Michael Bennet just announced that he’s running to be our next governor—and I wanted to reach out and share with you why I’m thrilled to support his campaign.
I have seen firsthand how Michael fights for Colorado in the U.S. Senate.
He has fought to expand access to healthcare, protect our environment, ensure future generations receive a high-quality education, and has the backs of Colorado workers.
Together, we’ve led countless efforts to prioritize this beautiful state we call home. We’ve worked on bills like the SMART Community Policing Act , which would protect our communities by supporting law enforcement and connecting Coloradans in crisis with mental health support, and the Protect the West Act , which would make critical investments in our forests to protect Colorado from the worst effects of climate change.
We have also worked together to make Colorado the best place for veterans, service members, and their families to live. We stood up to President Trump during his first term when he tried to rip Space Command out of Colorado, and we will continue to hold him accountable as he cozies up to dictators like Vladimir Putin, and his national security advisors, like Pete Hegseth and Tulsi Gabbard, put our troops at risk with their carelessness.
During these challenging times, Colorado needs a proven leader with Michael’s experience, values, and perseverance—that’s why I’m proud to endorse him for Governor.
I know Michael’s team is counting on grassroots supporters to keep this campaign moving forward. Friend, join me in supporting their early momentum by splitting a donation between my campaign and Michael’s today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act] DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act]
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act] DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act] OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfg_email-25q2_spl_jc?express_lane=true&refcode=X_250512_endorsement_spl_jc_act]
Together, we can show why Colorado is the best state in the country and provide the hopeful, optimistic vision we need.
Thank you,
Jason Crow

name: research@voteractionproject.org
subject: Supporter poll: Voter data verification system
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=s2w6OQK3hAlk7QstkWlW/KhbdQF9FmKwEpqlV/lz8cuaeta+2cQYDMEsZ3JAHTSjvmuC6ivai0k0iFP+fKIyYR6vNOvbGUje5Pp1u6z9Koo=]
CNN: Florida, West Virginia and Missouri withdraw from bipartisan effort aimed at maintaining accurate voter rolls [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/CNN_ERIC_Withdrawl.png]
The Electronic Registration Information Center, also known as ERIC, is a nonprofit system that helps participating states keep their registration rolls accurate and up to date by analyzing voter data and sharing reports with members , in order for them to update their voter rolls, remove ineligible voters and investigate potential voter fraud.
States from Florida, West Virginia and Missouri to Alabama, Louisiana and Texas withdrew in the last few years. Virginia withdrew as well, and after the state legislature passed a bill last year to rejoin, their governor, an opponent of voting rights, vetoed the bill. Critics of the withdrawals have argued that leaving ERIC will make it harder to verify that voter rolls are up to date in each state .
We want to hear from voters in your area about this tactic. Will you take our one-click poll right away?
Team: Will states regret leaving this nonprofit, nonpartisan voter data partnership?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=s2w6OQK3hAlk7QstkWlW/KhbdQF9FmKwEpqlV/lz8cuaeta+2cQYDMEsZ3JAHTSjvmuC6ivai0k0iFP+fKIyYR6vNOvbGUje5Pp1u6z9Koo=] NO [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=s2w6OQK3hAlk7QstkWlW/KhbdQF9FmKwEpqlV/lz8cuaeta+2cQYDMEsZ3JAHTSjvmuC6ivai0k0iFP+fKIyYR6vNOvbGUje5Pp1u6z9Koo=]
https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=s2w6OQK3hAlk7QstkWlW/KhbdQF9FmKwEpqlV/lz8cuaeta+2cQYDMEsZ3JAHTSjvmuC6ivai0k0iFP+fKIyYR6vNOvbGUje5Pp1u6z9Koo= [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=s2w6OQK3hAlk7QstkWlW/KhbdQF9FmKwEpqlV/lz8cuaeta+2cQYDMEsZ3JAHTSjvmuC6ivai0k0iFP+fKIyYR6vNOvbGUje5Pp1u6z9Koo=]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

clean_body: Voter Action Project [=] CNN: Florida, West Virginia and Missouri withdraw from bipartisan effort aimed at maintaining accurate voter rolls [] The Electronic Registration Information Center, also known as ERIC, is a nonprofit system that helps participating states keep their registration rolls accurate and up to date by analyzing voter data and sharing reports with members , in order for them to update their voter rolls, remove ineligible voters and investigate potential voter fraud. States from Florida, West Virginia and Missouri to Alabama, Louisiana and Texas withdrew in the last few years. Virginia withdrew as well, and after the state legislature passed a bill last year to rejoin, their governor, an opponent of voting rights, vetoed the bill. Critics of the withdrawals have argued that leaving ERIC will make it harder to verify that voter rolls are up to date in each state . We want to hear from voters in your area about this tactic. Will you take our one-click poll right away? Team: Will states regret leaving this nonprofit, nonpartisan voter data partnership? By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded: YES [=] NO [=] = [=] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? subject: Legislative Wrap Up
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF GLORIA JOHNSON, LISA SORENSEN, TREASURER
body:
The 2025 legislative session is over (but our fight continues)

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/f70b1a39da89/legislative-wrap-up?e=dc5f639d62)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
|
---
|
Peter
Last year, I crisscrossed this state—from Memphis to Mountain City—talking to working families about the challenges that keep them up at night. And do you know what I found? The working people of Knox County are worried about the exact same things as families in every corner of Tennessee: the rising cost of groceries, rent, and child care. Underfunded classrooms and crumbling roads. Lack of access to health care and the fear of gun violence in our communities.
Unfortunately, the controlling political party at our state Capitol isn’t doing a damn thing to solve any of it.
So when the 2025 legislative session of the 114th General Assembly began in January, Democrats came ready to get to work—focused on cutting costs, raising wages, and rebuilding Tennessee’s middle class.
Our caucus filed legislation to repeal the grocery tax, make housing and health care more affordable, and give every child access to universal pre-K and free school meals. We introduced a plan to reduce traffic by legalizing cannabis and dedicating the revenue to pay down the $39 billion backlog of road and bridge projects. We stood up for workers, seniors, gun safety, and civil rights.
I filed bills to raise the minimum wage, increase access to affordable child care, and establish a human right to birth control. I filed a red flag law that would give law enforcement the tools they need to keep guns away from people who pose a serious danger to themselves or others.
But Republicans had no interest in solving real problems.
Instead, they spent their time attacking immigrants, shielding gun manufacturers from accountability, and passing a corporate wish list—including a bill to let moneylenders jack up interest rates and fees on working families. They even eliminated a $60 million starter home fund that could have helped thousands of families buy their first home.
And the most damaging thing they did? Republicans passed a new statewide private school voucher scam that hands $150 million in taxpayer money to families already enrolled in private schools—at a time when our public schools rank 47th in the nation in per-pupil funding.
[**Chip in $25 now to help me keep fighting for Tennessee’s public schools and against this voucher scam.**](https://gmail.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=08ef345bf32fd78090e04097b&id=ff5a7bc160&e=dc5f639d62)
At one point this session, I stood to speak against a bill that would let government recruiters discriminate against well-qualified people of color. The Republican sponsor called it the “Dismantle DEI Act.” I filed an amendment to rename it the “White Fragility Act”—because we needed to say the quiet part out loud.
---
[|
---](https://gmail.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=08ef345bf32fd78090e04097b&id=9068ed7241&e=dc5f639d62)
| It's not anti-white to ensure that hiring decisions are based on merit and qualifications—not skin color. It’s about fairness. It’s about equity. And it’s about undoing the bias that still holds too many people back. It was gut-wrenching to watch my colleagues bastardize the meaning of DEI and unravel civil rights protections that Tennesseans have had for decades.
But even in Tennessee, progress is possible.
We stood alongside immigrant families, educators, and faith leaders to hold back a bill that would have denied children a public education based on immigration status.We blocked a bill that would have let international chemical companies escape legal responsibility when their products make people sick.
And we stopped legislation that would have let people openly carry semi-automatic rifles in public spaces—weapons that were made for a battlefield, not a ballfield.
[**If you're with me, chip in now. Every dollar helps us keep up the fight.**](https://gmail.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=08ef345bf32fd78090e04097b&id=2555e6dac6&e=dc5f639d62)
The 2025 legislative session is over. But the work is just beginning.
I’ll keep standing tall—for public schools, for civil rights, for reproductive freedom, and for every Tennessean who’s just trying to build a good life.
[**Make a donation today to help fuel this fight for the future our families deserve.**](https://gmail.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=08ef345bf32fd78090e04097b&id=1cab36ecc7&e=dc5f639d62)
It’s an honor to serve the people of Knox County and to fight for a Tennessee where every family has the freedom to thrive.
Thank you for reading—and thank you for standing with me.
Gloria JohnsonState Representative
House District 90
---
| PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF GLORIA JOHNSON, LISA SORENSEN, TREASURER

We’re running out of time to close our fundraising gap.
#

Hi there -- can we ask you to do something important for Gabby Giffords today and [**make a $3 contribution to GIFFORDS PAC?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250512-1-b&t=1&akid=10424%2E3935704%2EmiWeL5)
**[DONATE NOW](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250512-1-b&t=2&akid=10424%2E3935704%2EmiWeL5)**
We know you can’t give every time we ask, but right now, we’re still pacing behind where we need to be in order to reach the fundraising goals we’ve set for May. With just three days to go until our mid-quarter deadline, we’re running out of time to close our fundraising gap.
This is not a time when we can fall short. Our gun safety movement is facing attacks from Donald Trump and the gun lobby, and we need to do everything we can to make sure we have the resources to fight back.
[**Will you please make a $3 contribution to GIFFORDS PAC before Thursday? Gabby and all of us here are hoping we can count on our top supporters at this critical moment for our gun safety movement.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may25.7MLdP7&refcode=em250512-1-b&t=3&akid=10424%2E3935704%2EmiWeL5)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

****
Thank you,
\- Team GIFFORDS

# [10 Years Later: Surviving the Philadelphia Amtrak Crash ](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=163416447&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNDE2NDQ3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwNzM5OTEsImV4cCI6MTc0OTY2NTk5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jIqDhkMD4N-2JM_HlYrI_XE28o0TIIX8mryN6ysjc1g)
### Ten years ago, I survived a train crash that killed eight and injured over 200. I haven't stopped thinking about it since.
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| May 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=163416447&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNDE2NDQ3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0NzA3Mzk5MSwiZXhwIjoxNzQ5NjY1OTkxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.VdUyFJO29UhykxOqVuE4RQxedDYjG1lNdYpmE-lsRSI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=163416447&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNDE2NDQ3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwNzM5OTEsImV4cCI6MTc0OTY2NTk5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jIqDhkMD4N-2JM_HlYrI_XE28o0TIIX8mryN6ysjc1g&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=163416447&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzNDE2NDQ3LCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwNzM5OTEsImV4cCI6MTc0OTY2NTk5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jIqDhkMD4N-2JM_HlYrI_XE28o0TIIX8mryN6ysjc1g)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcGF0cmlja211cnBoeXBhL3AvMTAteWVhcnMtbGF0ZXItc3Vydml2aW5nLXRoZS1waGlsYWRlbHBoaWE_.Bn6Wspw-fTSkDDpt_03zV31Vr9AaV6zBELYosE_4I0k?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/10-years-later-surviving-the-philadelphia?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

If I had to guess, a [train derailment in Philadelphia ten years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/a3dee75d-44ca-4e55-b566-83673172c3a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) probably isn't top of mind for most people.
But I think about it every day because I didn't just read about that crash, [I crawled out of it.](https://substack.com/redirect/c3dc8f9e-c6be-4243-841b-24bc6fa864bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Once you've been launched sideways across a train car going over 100 miles an hour--after two combat tours and more than a few close calls--you start counting your lives like a cat. I think I'm on life number five.
It happened on May 12, 2015. A Tuesday evening.
Ten years ago today, that crash killed eight people and injured more than 200. Of the 243 passengers, I was one of the lucky ones. I walked away. Though "walked" might be generous. I crawled, bloodied and concussed, out of a twisted steel coffin.
That train ride--like so many moments in life--started normal and ended sideways and upside-down.
That morning began like any other. I was up at 5:30, home in Bucks County, PA. CrossFit at 6. I box jumped and then climbed a rope 21 times to a target two stories high. Got home, got my kids--Maggie, 8, and Jack, 5--onto the school bus. Then I was off to D.C. to tape an episode of my MSNBC show, _Taking the Hill_ , and meet with a candidate I was recruiting to join me at the Pentagon. At the time, I was being vetted by the White House _and_ the FBI to serve as Under Secretary of the Army. I'd served in Iraq and taught at West Point. This felt like my next chapter of public service.
The day felt productive. Life was busy, meaningful, and full.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/378e1919-8aea-4759-999e-485cc05b204a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
via The Washington Post
I felt gratitude for how far I'd come. When I served in Congress, I was one of the least wealthy members--out of 535, I was number 533. Great credit but still paying off my student loans and home mortgage. Almost there, even today!
I had no idea that by nightfall, another one of my nine lives would be spent.
That evening, I boarded the train home from D.C. and grabbed a seat in the cafe car. Across from me was Senator Tom Carper of Delaware. Sitting nearby was a 20-year-old Naval Academy midshipman named Justin Zemser. He was a former wide receiver and vice-president of the Jewish Midshipmen Club. Rockaway Beach kid. We joked--_Go Army, Beat Navy._ I offered to buy him a beer. He reminded me of the cadets I'd taught years earlier. I remember thinking: _This kid is going to lead something big someday._
A few hours later, Justin was dead.
I had my earbuds in, working on my iPad. Then, at 9:23 p.m., the train entered a sharp curve in Northeast Philadelphia at Frankford Junction going 100+ miles an hour, more than double the speed limit. The engineer, a 32-year-old new to the route, was distracted by emergency radio chatter from another train. He hit the emergency brake just moments too late and the train derailed like something out of a movie.
Two cars flipped and three rolled onto their sides. Metal twisted into unrecognizable shapes. Glass exploded. A man across from me had been replaced by a wall of steel. Another man next to me was unconscious. I slammed into the opposite wall, right where Senator Carper had been sitting. If he hadn't gotten off in Wilmington, I would have crushed him.
When I came to, I checked my arms and legs, then opened my eyes, I saw the moon through the place where the window was now the roof. There was blood. Screaming. And then, strangely, nothing. Just mostly moans, like the world was holding its breath.
Instead of leaving the wreckage, I decided to put my Army training to use and stayed back.
I shook the unconscious man beside me awake and got up onto a table still bolted to the floor.
When I was finally able to get myself up, I climbed through wreckage and got to work helping lift people up and out the window after reaching for the emergency lever and punching it open. Combat teaches you to compartmentalize. To not look back, focus on the next thing.
I started pulling people through what used to be the window. I used my tie as a tourniquet. I gave what I could: comfort, reassurance, directions to crawl toward light.
I told people help was coming--even when I didn't know if it was. I gave hope the way I was trained to.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a960a094-fbbc-4495-b466-d61dd178ce53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Aftermath of the derailment, via NBC4 Washington
I remember the stillness after the chaos, the silence before the sirens, the deep understanding that nothing would ever be the same again.
Eventually, first responders arrived. One of them--a firefighter I'd grown up with in Philly--shouted, "Murphy, is that you?" I told him who needed help and who was still alive in our train car.
Minutes later, my phone rang. It was Rachel Maddow. She asked if I was okay, and after I said I was, asked if I could go live on her show. I said yes, and then went on-air to report what had just happened from inside the wreckage until her hour was up and Lawrence O'Donnell took over.
Shortly after the call ended, so did my battery. Timing, as always, is everything.
When I finally got home, my wife Jenni was understandably furious. I hadn't called her. I didn't even think to. My phone had been dead. I was still in survival mode.
I said I was fine. But I wasn't.
The next night, coaching my son's hockey team, I forgot every kid's name. Names I'd known for years. I called one of them "27."
That's when Jenni knew something was wrong.
Traumatic brain injuries (TBI) are strange like that--they don't always scream. Sometimes they whisper. They hide in tiny cracks: in memory, in language, in emotions, parts of yourself you thought would never change and always be the same.
This was unlike anything I had ever experienced.
I'd survived Iraq, where 19 of my fellow soldiers didn't make it home. I'd had a gun pulled on me three times before I turned 18 growing up in Philly. And yet, for some reason, this--the concussion from the train crash--was the thing I was ashamed to admit. I couldn't even say it out loud. Not to Jenni. Not to my friends. Not to myself. It felt wrong to speak of injury when others didn't survive. I was lucky once again. Another case of survivors' guilt. So I did what so many do: I didn't talk about it. From the pain. From the symptoms. From the people trying to help.
Subscribe for free to read more stories like this.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wYXRyaWNrbXVycGh5cGEuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNjM0MTY0NDcsInMiOjQzOTkxMzMsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NDcwNzM5OTIsImV4cCI6MTc0OTY2NTk5MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.9f0gWpTX-lXboAStXgnidX-TVQgY0c-mt34kha9xnDs?)
A friend from the Army stepped in and insisted I see a doctor. I spent two days in a dark room. No phone. No stimulation. Just quiet. It helped. But I didn't talk about the crash--not for a long time. Even now, it still feels raw.
I've come to believe this: when moments of chaos hit, your habits either fail you or save you. That morning's rope climbs helped me physically climb out of a toppled train. The military drills gave me focus, discipline, and purpose.
Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. once said, "The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy." That quote lives inside me now.
Less than a year later, I was sworn in as the Acting Secretary of the Army--leading the very institution that taught me what to do when everything goes sideways. The _[Washington Post](https://substack.com/redirect/cbfcaae7-500c-4637-8c1b-187b24bf60eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/cbfcaae7-500c-4637-8c1b-187b24bf60eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)called me the "Soldier's Secretary." I led morning runs when visiting troops. Called cadence. I even rappelled down the side of the Fox News building live on air with the 101st Airborne while giving an interview.
But the public version of that story was only half of it.
What people didn't see were the quiet moments. The embarrassing memory lapses of people's names I've known for years, compensating with a "Yo Brother" or "Hey Sister" because I recognize their face but can't process their name. The weight of survivor's guilt once again. The neighbor who told the FBI when they knocked on his door while I was going through White House vetting that he didn't know me--"Nope, I don't know Patrick Murphy" because he was trying to help, thinking it might somehow protect me if I was in trouble (I love my neighborhood, people are so loyal).
Most people never see what you carry.
Some scars don't show up on X-rays. Some strength doesn't show up until it has to.
What saved me that night wasn't luck alone. It was habit. Muscle memory. The box jumps and 21 rope climbs I did that morning at CrossFit helped me climb out of a train car on its side.
In the military we have a phrase: "_Charlie Mike_." Continue the mission.
I still whisper it to myself.
I don't know why I survived when others didn't. That Navy Midshipman was the best of the best. I only know that I did. And because I did, I owe them something. Not just remembrance, but action. Integrity. Presence. A life that's lived with purpose. You don't get to choose the moment that tests you. But you do get to choose what you do with every breath. I'm still choosing to make a positive difference, every damn day.
And now I live like someone who somehow got more lives than they expected.
Maybe survival isn't about how many "cat lives" you lose, but how many new lives you can touch and make a difference in.
-Patrick
_The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter._
\- _**Honorable Patrick J. Murphy**_

clean_body: Ten years ago, I survived a train crash that killed eight and injured over 200. I haven't stopped thinking about it since. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Ten years ago, I survived a train crash that killed eight and injured over 200. I haven't stopped thinking about it since. | | | May 12 | | | | | | | | | | | | If I had to guess, a probably isn't top of mind for most people. But I think about it every day because I didn't just read about that crash, Once you've been launched sideways across a train car going over 100 miles an hour--after two combat tours and more than a few close calls--you start counting your lives like a cat. I think I'm on life number five. It happened on May 12, 2015. A Tuesday evening. Ten years ago today, that crash killed eight people and injured more than 200. Of the 243 passengers, I was one of the lucky ones. I walked away. Though "walked" might be generous. I crawled, bloodied and concussed, out of a twisted steel coffin. That train ride--like so many moments in life--started normal and ended sideways and upside-down. That morning began like any other. I was up at 5:30, home in Bucks County, PA. CrossFit at 6. I box jumped and then climbed a rope 21 times to a target two stories high. Got home, got my kids--Maggie, 8, and Jack, 5--onto the school bus. Then I was off to D.C. to tape an episode of my MSNBC show, Taking the Hill , and meet with a candidate I was recruiting to join me at the Pentagon. At the time, I was being vetted by the White House and the FBI to serve as Under Secretary of the Army. I'd served in Iraq and taught at West Point. This felt like my next chapter of public service. The day felt productive. Life was busy, meaningful, and full. | | ---|---|--- via The Washington Post I felt gratitude for how far I'd come. When I served in Congress, I was one of the least wealthy members--out of 535, I was number 533. Great credit but still paying off my student loans and home mortgage. Almost there, even today! I had no idea that by nightfall, another one of my nine lives would be spent. That evening, I boarded the train home from D.C. and grabbed a seat in the cafe car. Across from me was Senator Tom Carper of Delaware. Sitting nearby was a 20-year-old Naval Academy midshipman named Justin Zemser. He was a former wide receiver and vice-president of the Jewish Midshipmen Club. Rockaway Beach kid. We joked--Go Army, Beat Navy. I offered to buy him a beer. He reminded me of the cadets I'd taught years earlier. I remember thinking: This kid is going to lead something big someday. A few hours later, Justin was dead. I had my earbuds in, working on my iPad. Then, at 9:23 p.m., the train entered a sharp curve in Northeast Philadelphia at Frankford Junction going 100+ miles an hour, more than double the speed limit. The engineer, a 32-year-old new to the route, was distracted by emergency radio chatter from another train. He hit the emergency brake just moments too late and the train derailed like something out of a movie. Two cars flipped and three rolled onto their sides. Metal twisted into unrecognizable shapes. Glass exploded. A man across from me had been replaced by a wall of steel. Another man next to me was unconscious. I slammed into the opposite wall, right where Senator Carper had been sitting. If he hadn't gotten off in Wilmington, I would have crushed him. When I came to, I checked my arms and legs, then opened my eyes, I saw the moon through the place where the window was now the roof. There was blood. Screaming. And then, strangely, nothing. Just mostly moans, like the world was holding its breath. Instead of leaving the wreckage, I decided to put my Army training to use and stayed back. I shook the unconscious man beside me awake and got up onto a table still bolted to the floor. When I was finally able to get myself up, I climbed through wreckage and got to work helping lift people up and out the window after reaching for the emergency lever and punching it open. Combat teaches you to compartmentalize. To not look back, focus on the next thing. I started pulling people through what used to be the window. I used my tie as a tourniquet. I gave what I could: comfort, reassurance, directions to crawl toward light. I told people help was coming--even when I didn't know if it was. I gave hope the way I was trained to. | | ---|---|--- Aftermath of the derailment, via NBC4 Washington I remember the stillness after the chaos, the silence before the sirens, the deep understanding that nothing would ever be the same again. Eventually, first responders arrived. One of them--a firefighter I'd grown up with in Philly--shouted, "Murphy, is that you?" I told him who needed help and who was still alive in our train car. Minutes later, my phone rang. It was Rachel Maddow. She asked if I was okay, and after I said I was, asked if I could go live on her show. I said yes, and then went on-air to report what had just happened from inside the wreckage until her hour was up and Lawrence O'Donnell took over. Shortly after the call ended, so did my battery. Timing, as always, is everything. When I finally got home, my wife Jenni was understandably furious. I hadn't called her. I didn't even think to. My phone had been dead. I was still in survival mode. I said I was fine. But I wasn't. The next night, coaching my son's hockey team, I forgot every kid's name. Names I'd known for years. I called one of them "27." That's when Jenni knew something was wrong. Traumatic brain injuries (TBI) are strange like that--they don't always scream. Sometimes they whisper. They hide in tiny cracks: in memory, in language, in emotions, parts of yourself you thought would never change and always be the same. This was unlike anything I had ever experienced. I'd survived Iraq, where 19 of my fellow soldiers didn't make it home. I'd had a gun pulled on me three times before I turned 18 growing up in Philly. And yet, for some reason, this--the concussion from the train crash--was the thing I was ashamed to admit. I couldn't even say it out loud. Not to Jenni. Not to my friends. Not to myself. It felt wrong to speak of injury when others didn't survive. I was lucky once again. Another case of survivors' guilt. So I did what so many do: I didn't talk about it. From the pain. From the symptoms. From the people trying to help. Subscribe for free to read more stories like this. ? A friend from the Army stepped in and insisted I see a doctor. I spent two days in a dark room. No phone. No stimulation. Just quiet. It helped. But I didn't talk about the crash--not for a long time. Even now, it still feels raw. I've come to believe this: when moments of chaos hit, your habits either fail you or save you. That morning's rope climbs helped me physically climb out of a toppled train. The military drills gave me focus, discipline, and purpose. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. once said, "The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy." That quote lives inside me now. Less than a year later, I was sworn in as the Acting Secretary of the Army--leading the very institution that taught me what to do when everything goes sideways. The me the "Soldier's Secretary." I led morning runs when visiting troops. Called cadence. I even rappelled down the side of the Fox News building live on air with the 101st Airborne while giving an interview. But the public version of that story was only half of it. What people didn't see were the quiet moments. The embarrassing memory lapses of people's names I've known for years, compensating with a "Yo Brother" or "Hey Sister" because I recognize their face but can't process their name. The weight of survivor's guilt once again. The neighbor who told the FBI when they knocked on his door while I was going through White House vetting that he didn't know me--"Nope, I don't know Patrick Murphy" because he was trying to help, thinking it might somehow protect me if I was in trouble (I love my neighborhood, people are so loyal). Most people never see what you carry. Some scars don't show up on X-rays. Some strength doesn't show up until it has to. What saved me that night wasn't luck alone. It was habit. Muscle memory. The box jumps and 21 rope climbs I did that morning at CrossFit helped me climb out of a train car on its side. In the military we have a phrase: "Charlie Mike." Continue the mission. I still whisper it to myself. I don't know why I survived when others didn't. That Navy Midshipman was the best of the best. I only know that I did. And because I did, I owe them something. Not just remembrance, but action. Integrity. Presence. A life that's lived with purpose. You don't get to choose the moment that tests you. But you do get to choose what you do with every breath. I'm still choosing to make a positive difference, every damn day. And now I live like someone who somehow got more lives than they expected. Maybe survival isn't about how many "cat lives" you lose, but how many new lives you can touch and make a difference in. -Patrick The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter. \- Honorable Patrick J. Murphy | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Patrick J. Murphy ? ?

name: MassDems
subject: Brian "Slowzone" Shortsleeve is Wrong for Massachusetts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Peter-
Another Trump loyalist just joined the race for Governor of Massachusetts.
Brian Shortsleeve - who voted for Donald Trump and chaired Ron DeSantis’s presidential campaign - announced his bid for the Republican nomination. His record is clear: he supported policies that threatened health care for seniors and working families, slashed research funding and pushed economic chaos through reckless tariffs. That’s not leadership—it's damage.
Let’s not forget: Brian Shortsleeve left the MBTA in crisis. Under his watch, service declined and safety suffered. Riders were left with breakdowns, slowzones, delays and dangerous conditions. Today, thanks to Governor Maura Healey and GM Phil Eng, the MBTA is free of slow zones for the first time in years. That turnaround didn’t happen under Shortsleeve. It happened despite him.
Watch this video to learn more about how Massachusetts needs to say NO to Brian “Slowzone” Shortsleeve:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gCaPwj16HBE [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gCaPwj16HBE]
“Slowzone” Shortsleeve can’t run from his record. He brought dysfunction to public transit and stood behind Trump every step of the way. We can’t afford that kind of leadership in the Governor’s office.
Massachusetts deserves better. Let’s keep moving forward—not backwards.
- MassDems

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Words I never imagined I’d say…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2508?t=1&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
Here’s something I never thought I’d say, null:
**The sitting president has given the richest man in the world access to our most personal information – to do who knows what with. This includes things like our Social Security numbers and bank account information.**
If that totally freaks you out, I’m right there with you.
That’s why I introduced a bill in the House to _block_ Elon Musk’s access to our private data. And I’m running to become Michigan’s next senator to take back the majority for Democrats and stop Trump and Musk from inflicting even more chaos and harm on everyday Americans.
But I need to level with you: **Election experts predict that Michigan will likely decide Senate control, which means the full force of the GOP is going to be coming after this seat to flip it red. I can’t fight back against that kind of spending without your grassroots support.**
**[That’s why I’m humbly asking today: Will you chip in $5 or more now to fuel my campaign to victory in Michigan, take back our Democratic Senate majority, and protect the American people from Donald Trump and Elon Musk?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2509?t=2&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2510?t=3&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2511?t=4&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2512?t=5&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2509?t=6&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
Your support means the world. Thank you.

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** Null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you chip in now to keep Michigan blue and take back the Senate for Democrats?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2508?t=7&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/2508?t=8&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from this email list, please click [here](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=9&akid=111%2E49239%2E2ltaDn).
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Amarillo, Texas
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

I held a town hall meeting in Amarillo on Saturday. In a county that voted
80% for Donald Trump, more than 700 people showed up — Democrats,
Republicans (in Trump hats too) and Independents. It speaks to the urgency
of the moment and the fact that people are looking past party
identification for something better for this country.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250512.0UnEI6&refcode=ema20250512-240112&t=1001&akid=240112%2E444211%2E4CsLWa ]Image of the Amarillo Town Hall. It is in an indoor ballroom. The room
is full of people, seated. The POV is on the edge of the room so you can
see the rows of people stretching out towards the speaker in the center of
the room.

We talked about big cuts to the VA, to Medicaid and Social Security.
School teachers talked about dwindling resources amidst growing need and
how it’s going to get even tougher with Greg Abbott’s vouchers draining
even more money from local schools.

We listened to each other’s stories, and committed to look out for one
another. A young mother told us about how cuts to the program that
supports kids with disabilities is going to hurt her 12-year-old daughter
who goes to a school in Canyon, Texas. I asked her when the next school
board meeting is at Canyon ISD — she told us next Monday at 7pm. I then
asked those in attendance — how many of you are willing to join her at
that meeting to have her back and ask the trustees to support the programs
that help her daughter? Hands shot up around the room.

I’m going to stay on the road — holding town halls no matter how Red or
Blue the county. We can’t afford to write anyone off or take anyone for
granted. This country is in crisis, and the only way forward is to show up
— and get to work.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250512.0UnEI6&refcode=ema20250512-240112&t=1002&akid=240112%2E444211%2E4CsLWa ]If you can, please donate $3 to make sure we have the
resources to do this work. I don’t take a salary from Powered by People
but everything else, like putting gas in the tank, renting event spaces,
etc. costs money. Please chip in today if you can afford to.

Keep the faith and keep up the fight,

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=240112%2E444211%2E4CsLWa

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

**Not only will this race be one of the most competitive and consequential in the country, it’ll be one of the most expensive.**

We spent down to the wire last November in our successful efforts to flip this seat. Now, we’re focusing on rebuilding our warchest early as we prepare for the GOP attacks to come.

clean_body: | = We need your eyes on this: Roll Call just released their list of the top ten most vulnerable incumbents in Congress. Derek ranks #5 overall, and he’s the #2 most vulnerable Democrat in the entire country. = Derek sent shockwaves through Washington when we flipped CA-45 from red to blue. It was one of the closest margins nationwide, just 653 votes put us over the top. It’s no secret why he’s already an early Republican target. = | = With only three seats separating House control, CA-45 will make or break the majority – and Democrats’ ability to hold the Trump Administration accountable. Not only will this race be one of the most competitive and consequential in the country, it’ll be one of the most expensive. We spent down to the wire last November in our successful efforts to flip this seat. Now, we’re focusing on rebuilding our warchest early as we prepare for the GOP attacks to come. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Team Tran | www.derektranforcongress.com Use of Derek Tran's military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Dept. of Defense or U.S. Army. clean_body: I don't hand out positions like this lightly. When we find someone who stands strong, who has the guts to fight for what's right, we remember it. You've done just that, Peter. That 's why, when the final seat on the Republican Advisory Board came up, I said to hold it. ** **** This Board is not about titles, it's about doing the work. It's about protecting the progress we have made and making sure our majority stays strong. I should know - I 'm a proud member myself! **** This seat was held for you for a reason. You've earned it, now let's finish the job. For the Constitution and the country, Jim Jordan United States Congressman **** Paid for by the Republican National Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

# MARCELL STRBICH FOR OHIO SECRETARY OF STATE!

OFFICIAL CAMPAIGN KICKOFF!

YOU'RE INVITED TO THIS EXCITING, FREE, EVENT!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=856a0d8e3c&e=5c740fa3e2)
---
|
---
| **MARCELL STRBICH OFFICIAL CAMPAIGN KICKOFF EVENT!
MAY 21, 2024 at 7:00 PM • MEDINA, OHIO**
We have a grassroots, patriot, combat veteran who has stepped up to selflessly run for Ohio Secretary of State in 2026, Marcell Strbich. Many of you know Marcell. You've heard him on the radio with Bob Frantz, Jack Windsor, and Brian Thomas. You've seen him on video with Emerald Robinson and Steve Stern. You've seen him testify at the State Capital on election integrity legislation. He can and will do the job to secure Ohio's elections and clean up Ohio's registered voter rolls, which contrary to what you hear from our current Secretary of State, are a MESS. Many of you know Marcell and personally endorse him. For those of you who don't, once you get to know him we're confident he'll have your endorsement too!
**Marcell is officially kicking off his campaign the evening of May 21st in Medina. It's a FREE event and all are welcome! Come meet Marcell, ask him questions, and get answers!**
**To learn more about Marcell Strbich, go[HERE](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=d189985d68&e=5c740fa3e2) or click on the image below.

[](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=4e66c03889&e=5c740fa3e2)**
**Here's a video of what we all need to do. HINT: It's titled "DONATE • DONATE • DONATE!" and you can do it[ HERE](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=6dff3d3ef9&e=5c740fa3e2).**
---
| [ ](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=b5592bf91f&e=5c740fa3e2 "Donate • Donate • Donate! Marcell Strbich for Ohio Secretary of State!")
---
DONATE • DONATE • DONATE! MARCELL STRBICH FOR OHIO SoS
|
---
| |
---
|
---
| |
# FREE TRAINING FOR SCHOOL BOARD CANDIDATES!
---
| [ ](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=8b254d72c7&e=5c740fa3e2)
---
| **CANDIDATE TRAINING ALERT: HOW TO RUN FOR YOUR SCHOOL BOARD
WEDNESDAY MAY 14th at 7:00 PM by ZOOM!
JOIN IN YOUR SWEATPANTS!**
---
| Our good friends at **OSBCC** , the **Ohio School Board Constitutional Coalition** , are holding a **FREE training session for school board candidates** next week! They asked us to help them get the word out. **PLEASE SHARE THIS WITH ANYONE & EVERYONE YOU KNOW WHO IS RUNNING OR MIGHT BE CONSIDERING RUNNING FOR SCHOOL BOARD.**

"Thinking about running for school board? Ohio School Board Constitutional Coalition ([OSBCC.org](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=f68c646795&e=5c740fa3e2)) can help you run your best campaign possible! During the 2023 election cycle, **about 40% of the candidates OSBCC worked with were elected** , and they think they can help even more in 2025!

**The first training session is on Wednesday, May 14 at 7 PM via Zoom.** To get the training link and other details, **become a member of OSBCC (FREE)** by going to [https://osbcc.org/membership-application/](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=8103d7bb76&e=5c740fa3e2) and fill out your contact information and sign the OSBCC pledge. An email will be sent out with all the training details. **Running for school board is critical to America's future, and there have never been more resources to help you get elected!"**
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | **GFI NEWSLETTER ARCHIVE**
---
|
[**CLICK HERE for access to all past issues!**](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=ae78ddb60b&e=5c740fa3e2)

---
|
---
| Thank you for being a GFI **person** as part of the GFI **network**. We're always interested in how we can do a better job of connecting patriots across Ohio. If you want to communicate, we're here!

Best regards,

**Mark Pukita
A GFI Person
mark@gfiohio.com
614-499-0599 (c)
[GFIOhio.com](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=33977bbe56&e=5c740fa3e2)**
---
| | _Copyright © 2025 The Grassroots Freedom Initiative, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
The Grassroots Freedom Initiative4240 Hobbs Landing Dr WDublin, OH 43017-6025
[Add us to your address book](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/vcard?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/profile?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31&e=5c740fa3e2&c=3ebd1243c9) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://gfiohio.us17.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c2cee5a5358289d5dd20ff17d&id=9877b1cc31&t=b&e=5c740fa3e2&c=3ebd1243c9).

* **TOP NEWS**

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Faces Major Housing Market Correction as Prices Drop Across State** ," Newsweek's **James Bickerton** \-- "The Texas housing market is undergoing a substantial correction, driven by a combination of oversupply, declining demand and persistent affordability issues.

"We need to talk about Texas. Listings just hit 123,000 in April 2025. 53 percent higher than normal. And prices are now dropping across the state," **Nick Gerti** , a real estate analyst and the CEO of Reventure App, wrote on X, formerly [Twitter](https://substack.com/redirect/390c3157-e497-4624-b7fa-f09d08516ee9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), on Thursday.

He warned that Texas is now the fourth most [oversupplied housing market](https://substack.com/redirect/102a97a7-7c0f-43f9-b656-a9007788b67d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the United States.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/7eaeb306-efb6-43a9-855e-11b88e3c8c1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_Newsweek_ contacted the Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office for comment on Friday via email outside of regular office hours.

Texas experienced a significant migration boom during the pandemic, attracting new residents with the promise of affordability, space and lower taxes. In 2022, net domestic migration brought 222,100 new residents to the state. However, by 2024, that number fell to 85,200, a 62 percent decline, according to Gerli.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/7eaeb306-efb6-43a9-855e-11b88e3c8c1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Additionally, [Texas led the nation in homebuilding](https://substack.com/redirect/f6cd97d9-20cd-4194-8f5c-d35a078854bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), issuing 15 percent of the country's new-home permits in 2024. But as population growth slowed and high mortgage rates locked out potential buyers, the increased supply outpaced demand, resulting in downward pressure on prices in major metropolitan areas such as Dallas, Houston and San Antonio.

Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market.

"Values are down -0.7 percent over the last year, and have dropped -1.6 percent from the middle of 2022," Gerli wrote on X, adding in a separate post. "Buyer demand keeps dropping, and listings keep rising."" "We need to talk about Texas. Listings just hit 123,000 in April 2025. 53 percent higher than normal. And prices are now dropping across the state," Nick Gerti , a real estate analyst and the CEO of Reventure App, wrote on X, formerly , on Thursday. He warned that Texas is now the fourth most in the United States. Newsweek contacted the Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office for comment on Friday via email outside of regular office hours. Texas experienced a significant migration boom during the pandemic, attracting new residents with the promise of affordability, space and lower taxes. In 2022, net domestic migration brought 222,100 new residents to the state. However, by 2024, that number fell to 85,200, a 62 percent decline, according to Gerli. Additionally, , issuing 15 percent of the country's new-home permits in 2024. But as population growth slowed and high mortgage rates locked out potential buyers, the increased supply outpaced demand, resulting in downward pressure on prices in major metropolitan areas such as Dallas, Houston and San Antonio. Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market. "Values are down -0.7 percent over the last year, and have dropped -1.6 percent from the middle of 2022," Gerli wrote on X, adding in a separate post. In 2022, net domestic migration brought 222,100 new residents to the state. However, by 2024, that number fell to 85,200, a 62 percent decline, according to Gerli.

Additionally, [Texas led the nation in homebuilding](https://substack.com/redirect/d4978d53-02fc-408c-a6d3-a0861e34c12e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), issuing 15 percent of the country's new-home permits in 2024. But as population growth slowed and high mortgage rates locked out potential buyers, the increased supply outpaced demand, resulting in downward pressure on prices in major metropolitan areas such as Dallas, Houston and San Antonio.

Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market.

"Values are down -0.7 percent over the last year, and have dropped -1.6 percent from the middle of 2022," Gerli wrote on X, adding in a separate post. "Buyer demand keeps dropping, and listings keep rising."" TOP NEWS "Texas Faces Major Housing Market Correction as Prices Drop Across State ," Newsweek's James Bickerton \-- "The Texas housing market is undergoing a substantial correction, driven by a combination of oversupply, declining demand and persistent affordability issues. "We need to talk about Texas. Listings just hit 123,000 in April 2025. 53 percent higher than normal. And prices are now dropping across the state," Nick Gerti , a real estate analyst and the CEO of Reventure App, wrote on X, formerly , on Thursday. He warned that Texas is now the fourth most in the United States. Newsweek contacted the Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office for comment on Friday via email outside of regular office hours. Texas experienced a significant migration boom during the pandemic, attracting new residents with the promise of affordability, space and lower taxes. In 2022, net domestic migration brought 222,100 new residents to the state. However, by 2024, that number fell to 85,200, a 62 percent decline, according to Gerli. Additionally, , issuing 15 percent of the country's new-home permits in 2024. But as population growth slowed and high mortgage rates locked out potential buyers, the increased supply outpaced demand, resulting in downward pressure on prices in major metropolitan areas such as Dallas, Houston and San Antonio. Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market. "Values are down -0.7 percent over the last year, and have dropped -1.6 percent from the middle of 2022," Gerli wrote on X, adding in a separate post. "Buyer demand keeps dropping, and listings keep rising."" FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries. Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market.

Join thousands of subscribers from across the political spectrum who trust Ground News to keep them informed, not outraged. | TOP NEWS "Texas Faces Major Housing Market Correction as Prices Drop Across State ,” Newsweek's James Bickerton \-- "The Texas housing market is undergoing a substantial correction, driven by a combination of oversupply, declining demand and persistent affordability issues. "We need to talk about Texas. Listings just hit 123,000 in April 2025. 53 percent higher than normal. And prices are now dropping across the state," Nick Gerti , a real estate analyst and the CEO of Reventure App, wrote on X, formerly , on Thursday. He warned that Texas is now the fourth most in the United States. » Newsweek contacted the Texas Economic Development & Tourism Office for comment on Friday via email outside of regular office hours. Texas experienced a significant migration boom during the pandemic, attracting new residents with the promise of affordability, space and lower taxes. In 2022, net domestic migration brought 222,100 new residents to the state. However, by 2024, that number fell to 85,200, a 62 percent decline, according to Gerli. » Additionally, , issuing 15 percent of the country's new-home permits in 2024. But as population growth slowed and high mortgage rates locked out potential buyers, the increased supply outpaced demand, resulting in downward pressure on prices in major metropolitan areas such as Dallas, Houston and San Antonio. Gerli argued the housing inventory increase is primarily fueled by three factors: a wave of resale of newly constructed homes, a decline in migration into the state and a growing number of locals being priced out of the market. "Values are down -0.7 percent over the last year, and have dropped -1.6 percent from the middle of 2022," Gerli wrote on X, adding in a separate post. "Buyer demand keeps dropping, and listings keep rising."" | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. name: Working Families Party
subject: TONIGHT: Emergency call with Mayor Baraka and members of Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | You may have already seen the news, Peter. On Friday, ICE agents arrested Newark Mayor and WFP-endorsed candidate for NJ Governor **Ras Baraka,** while he was visiting a newly re-opened detention center — a visit that was entirely within his rights and oversight responsibilities. But it didn’t stop there. Alongside him were members of Congress — **Rep. Bonnie Watson Coleman** , **Rep. Rob Menendez Jr.** , and **Rep. LaMonica McIver** — who were shoved, manhandled, and physically confronted by ICE agents. They weren’t arrested on the spot, but they’re now facing threats of arrest simply for being there. Let that sink in, Peter: A sitting mayor, _arrested_. Members of Congress, _assaulted_ and _threatened_ by federal agents. All for demanding transparency from an agency that operates behind closed doors. **_This_****is how authoritarianism takes hold** — when elected leaders are punished for doing their jobs, and federal agents are used to intimidate anyone who dares stand up for the people. This wasn’t just an attack on Mayor Baraka and three members of Congress. It was an attack on our democracy. [**Tonight at 5:00 PM ET, join an emergency mass call with Rep. Rob Menendez Jr., Rep. Bonnie Watson Coleman, Rep. LaMonica McIver, and Mayor Ras Baraka himself. They’ll break down what happened, what it means, and how we can fight back.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdLYKALSS51m8VcipjtURaCTZ6kgFC1kaug92UaadBiI6aUkuGFCkCj2igWB2j0sxCj0bZ2pgjRQHwUNL7Zv37OQdpNFohutL8q465DwZ0UOC81JcI7QY4la8mMuv7mxxXI7IXaShjfA1Fs3Rzle2p95Ob0jLn5L_rOHVgENHSH70zO9ZQh_qdo_uLj9siMhGmGuCzzjB0vnHKdr42EZFkgSlvE_ucsl2sLzACw8QjRw-mil_VaWyfPwvRahVUXArrhraqxvxVZfryVsVZayLur2IiDJPGwVOBvBi43nEz8vLQ/4gf/dCY9mAXKTfCkMnTMwqRmjQ/h0/h001.obSj1P85yv_EriuJMWx_dnwlA3SWQo11MPN8SFISqrQ) **[RSVP now »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdLYKALSS51m8VcipjtURaCTZ6kgFC1kaug92UaadBiI6aUkuGFCkCj2igWB2j0sxCi2f-SmWl1Wo9Gh7gF-ugwXcXknJIj8oBDHlVHWgHreGTKRRSUPsVqu2LiN392uvHGV_Ujsk9zT_USHRdNeoYFobrb0z-8YWkV3Wb6LOEUdyG5W8i_tVfvk5MgnaS685b1NJbBNA65neWLMBSljsa1j1KzUwJKup-Qldn0hHz5w-U-59daJuhiVqEIrs-0MHE5vjVLxacTj8qsB_PTs-KhbTQr9ubyUGEnMBQRZA9_68w/4gf/dCY9mAXKTfCkMnTMwqRmjQ/h1/h001.uZn9HTj55bncQhPNkheFTboeq2uIgTXaMOyBiCpe7TU)** This call is open to anyone who refuses to accept this kind of intimidation as the new normal. body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107568562/545102100/1561815853?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDBmMjdkZC02MzJmLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B6oahLCNWsiJ-vx9EB3dtHGbyzbn-4IrEtZM_OAUyY4=&emci=475d8abd-3f21-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=2d0f27dd-632f-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=12666102) Hey Peter,

Since Yadira launched her campaign to win back Colorado’s 8th District, we’ve been overwhelmed by the amount of support we’ve received from people like you – and we couldn’t be more grateful.

Every day that we grow our movement gets us another day closer to flipping this seat and taking back the House in 2026. Here in Minnesota, we’ve joined separate litigation alongside 18 other states to stop the rest of this executive order. We’re not backing down.
This ruling was clear: no president, regardless of party, can short-circuit the will of Congress and unilaterally change how our elections work.
We’ve seen this before. The SAVE Act, which was passed by the House, tried to push these same harmful restrictions. And now the Trump administration is trying to implement them by executive order.
[**This is a legal, political, and moral battle—and I will never stop defending the right to vote for every eligible American. Please help me keep up the fight by chipping in $5 or $10 now →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107568651/545102394/-76723109?refcode2=feb43730-642f-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&amount=25&refcode=ss_em_fr_90DO_250512&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDAzNTUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmViNDM3MzAtNjQyZi1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=FjOk2KIk5Gw0XnBuWoyoTChEfuD03_PaXh6JvZm2rts=)

body:
In a surprise announcement, Kayla Tausche, CNN’s senior White House correspondent, is parting ways with the network just two years after being recruited from CNBC in what was once hailed as a major journalistic coup. Her departure adds to a growing list of high-profile exits from the embattled network, which is grappling with plummeting viewership, [...] | [](http://trk.libertyledger.)

# **[Kayla Tausche, Leaves CNN](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
In a surprise announcement, Kayla Tausche, CNN’s senior White House correspondent, is parting ways with the network just two years after being recruited from CNBC in what was once hailed as a major journalistic coup. Her departure adds to a growing list of high-profile exits from the embattled network, which is grappling with plummeting viewership, [...]
[**Read More**](http://trk.libertyledger.)
[](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9NjkxMDkifQ/MJhZVsHr9XxXkhR6Gui_-w)
# **[Social Media Responds To Prevost's Social Media Posts](http://trk.libertyledger._c2V5aWQ9NjkxMDkifQ/MJhZVsHr9XxXkhR6Gui_-w)**
---
The media frenzy is reaching biblical proportions — and it’s not because of anything Pope Leo XIV has said. body:
| | | **Republican Medicaid cuts would harm the families I help as a social worker. I’m running a red to blue race in VA-HD22 to defend access to health care.**
**[Sign the petition: NO cuts to Medicaid!](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38YIS_P_YcOYCCdM10KghjssohM6sX_0_ihbDiS78NuL0l4y65_IfwpbD_zSF6G9qNESFZXE9ddgloiOWS4bvT9PUixZzB38EJlx3Qe_NZPxcDeeEJRX4x-VfJKxhR7mTHToGk466ssZah947FQf-Zhk8rJehl_e0Eg5qvrdVcF28YNmOb8uUlMvBLqZyaQFGYHB0GZP0rwgcWMkayTa0dgZEdbVgrQjOQr5yFoZhLAtTDWt_81KxBq6jRS8yL-dKYhKns-TOEIbtyLFNgNMLiN/4gf/lnLFdwzBR92bQC2FL_ydZg/h0/h001.H89fPYZkRT1HLNw4sLO1EzjpylZa-WCGhdtwerQfaDY)**
* * *
This week, Republicans in Congress are working on devastating cuts to Medicaid, threatening access to health care for over 1.8 million Virginians. As a former Virginia State Delegate, I voted to expand Medicaid across the state to working families - and I won’t let Republicans in Washington cut Medicaid without a fight.
Medicaid is a federal and state partnership.
If Republicans in Washington slash Medicaid funding to give tax cuts to wealthy billionaires, our states will have to pick up the slack ... or kick millions of families out of coverage altogether.
**I’m Elizabeth Guzman: a mom, social worker, and immigrant running to return to the Virginia House of Delegates this November against a MAGA Republican.**
Grassroots donors like you have made over _3,000 donations_ to our campaign to protect our health care - will you stand with us?
**[Sign the petition: NO cuts to Medicaid!](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38YIS_P_YcOYCCdM10KghjssohM6sX_0_ihbDiS78NuLzZRZdc1L8UQXD0Pm6Otszx2jr6gAMFQpQzONmyUdbMFooc4c7AHfAbMDG4czMgRNIf8wf6Zg3_2FQPUE5k1tQF8SP4LuPZYl_A4BV9efNkdvsCHx6dtLUNmJLnENW6n9BYakbje3gfG9CSMRK0EW907mYGhpcqOlU7Z2Qa12j2MfsYwwmWGCzXI4yjoezGcnDu0qU__gv4qaOzO2BqOZRXUNsHO5rkYaj2ZmUCQAFbz/4gf/lnLFdwzBR92bQC2FL_ydZg/h1/h001.hO5kPftett_2xdDJr0V1o_dW5Eu2osvvPVn4fTUpZuQ)** [SIGN ON](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm38YIS_P_YcOYCCdM10KghjssohM6sX_0_ihbDiS78NuL3t-KfY7YJDp7h-qw08BUT9fOef4Ps823uYoNTdGBxLZkQcqRZ5XgE659GR22DtBWzQWpKmgFXI0uKB7yLn_UeKdKKnZzG84RzpP1vreDq8I0Y8rnkalRJg6X49ydDJOKlmrI-tk8aRPArTxpzwL9p8pcuYuDa8ewm0-dpDGlbNRdSL_IDZM3oVl0C5H2XLnMJmKUstA18gf7sZxUuK0No5gWjhI-HQ9vEiI2K2pLSi-/4gf/lnLFdwzBR92bQC2FL_ydZg/h2/h001.pOGQtwHKMuYe1gnKekV4gtvhrYPxul107XOUiyPYkag)

Elizabeth Guzman
